abnormally.definition: In an abnormal manner; in a way that deviates from a standard, norm, or average.
abnormally.exampleZh: 託拜厄斯看起來異常瘦弱。

abolish.definition: To end a law, system, institution, custom or practice.
abolish.example: Slavery was abolished in the nineteenth century.
abolish.exampleZh: 奴隸制於十九世紀被廢除。

abandoned.definition: Having given oneself up to vice; immoral; extremely wicked, or sinning without restraint; irreclaimably wicked.

aboriginal.definition: Of or pertaining to Australian Aboriginal peoples, Aborigines, or their language.
aboriginal.exampleZh: 丹尼爾使用傳統的原住民藥物。

abruptly.definition: In an abrupt manner; without giving notice, or without the usual forms; suddenly; precipitously.
abruptly.example: The professor stopped her lecture abruptly when she noticed someone fall off their seat.
abruptly.exampleZh: 當教授發現有人從座位上摔下來時，她突然停止了演講。

cloak.example: Night hid her movements with its cloak of darkness.
cloak.exampleZh: 夜晚用黑暗的斗篷隱藏了她的行動。

exterior.definition: The outside part, parts or surface of something.
exterior.example: The sticker was attached to the exterior of the package

timid.example: John's a very timid person. I'll doubt he'll be brave enough to face his brother.
timid.exampleZh: 約翰是一個非常膽怯的人。我懷疑他是否有足夠的勇氣面對他的兄弟。

reluctantly.definition: In a reluctant or hesitant manner.
reluctantly.exampleZh: 米羅斯拉夫無奈地同意了。

complexity.definition: The state of being complex; intricacy; entanglement.

envisage.definition: To conceive or see something within one's mind; to imagine or envision.
envisage.example: I envisage such a time when I could order a Schnitzel without disdain for my bad German.
envisage.exampleZh: 我想像有一天我可以點一份炸肉排，而不必鄙視我糟糕的德語。

exude.definition: To discharge through pores or incisions, as moisture or other liquid matter; to give out.

headmistress.example: The headmistress presented the new teacher.

mien.definition: Demeanor; facial expression or attitude, especially one which is intended by its bearer.
mien.example: His avuncular mien endears him to small children.
mien.exampleZh: 他慈祥的舉止深受小朋友的喜愛。

timidity.exampleZh: 弗拉基米爾膽怯地回答。

abuse.definition: Improper treatment or usage; application to a wrong or bad purpose; an unjust, corrupt or wrongful practice or custom.
abuse.example: All abuse, whether physical, verbal, psychological or sexual, is bad.
abuse.exampleZh: 所有虐待，無論是身體上的、言語上的、心理上的還是性的，都是不好的。

absurd.definition: Contrary to reason or propriety; obviously and flatly opposed to manifest truth; inconsistent with the plain dictates of common sense; logically contradictory; nonsensical; ridiculous; silly.

absentee.definition: A person who is absent from his or her employment, school, post, duty, etc.
absentee.example: The absentee landlord requires a state to enforce his claims to the property.
absentee.exampleZh: 缺席房東要求州強制執行他對財產的主張。

accommodation.definition: (usually a mass noun) Lodging in a dwelling or similar living quarters afforded to travellers in hotels or on cruise ships, or prisoners, etc.

accessibility.definition: The quality of being accessible, or of admitting approach; receptiveness.
accessibility.example: The word "accessibility" is commonly used in formal English.

accumulate.definition: To heap up in a mass; to pile up; to collect or bring together (either literally or figuratively)
accumulate.example: He wishes to accumulate a sum of money.

achievable.definition: Capable of being achieved, which either means possible or probable.
achievable.example: The word "achievable" is commonly used in formal English.
achievable.exampleZh: 「可實現」一詞在正式英語中常用。

ache.definition: To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.

actual.definition: Existing in reality, not just potentially; really acted or acting; occurring in fact.
actual.example: The actual government expenses dramatically exceed the budget.
actual.exampleZh: 政府的實際支出大大超出了預算。

acquisition.definition: The act or process of acquiring.
acquisition.example: The acquisition of sports equipment can be fun in itself.
acquisition.exampleZh: 購買運動器材本身就很有趣。

addict.definition: A person who is addicted, especially to a harmful drug
addict.example: He is an addict when it comes to chocolate cookies.

addiction.definition: A state that is characterized by compulsive drug use or compulsive engagement in rewarding behavior, despite negative consequences.

adaptable.definition: Capable of adapting or of being adapted.

adequate.definition: Equal to or fulfilling some requirement.

additionally.definition: By way of addition; in addition to; also.
additionally.exampleZh: 安東尼奧進行了額外的研究。

admirable.definition: Deserving of the highest esteem or admiration.
admirable.example: It's admirable that Shelley overcame her handicap and excelled in her work.
admirable.exampleZh: 雪萊克服了自己的障礙並在工作中表現出色，令人欽佩。

adjustment.definition: The settling or balancing of a financial account
adjustment.example: The credit card company made an adjustment to my account to waive the late fee.
adjustment.exampleZh: 信用卡公司對我的帳戶進行了調整，免除了滯納金。

administrative.definition: Of or relating to administering or administration.

adequately.exampleZh: 天王星尚未得到充分探測。

admiringly.example: The word "admiringly" is commonly used in formal English.

admittedly.definition: As is acknowledged to be true; by general admission; confessedly.
admittedly.example: Admittedly, vandalism is a bit of a problem.
admittedly.exampleZh: 誠然，破壞公物是一個問題。

adoption.definition: The state of being adopted; the acceptance of a child of other parents as if he or she were one's own child.
adoption.example: A Chinese baby girl was given away for adoption.
adoption.exampleZh: 一名中國女嬰被送人收養。

adopted.definition: To take or receive as one's own what is not so naturally.
adopted.example: He adopted a new look in order to fit in with his new workmates.
adopted.exampleZh: 為了適應新同事，他換了新的面貌。

adore.definition: To love with one's entire heart and soul; regard with deep respect and affection.
adore.example: It is obvious to everyone that Gerry adores Heather.
adore.exampleZh: 大家都知道，格里很喜歡希瑟。

adorn.definition: To make more beautiful and attractive; to decorate.
adorn.example: a character adorned with every Christian grace
adorn.exampleZh: 一個充滿基督教恩典的人物

advance.definition: A forward move; improvement or progression.
advance.example: an advance in health or knowledge

advancement.definition: The act of advancing, ; promotion to a higher place or dignity

advent.definition: Arrival; onset; a time when something first comes or appears
advent.example: A chocolate is hidden behind each door of this Advent calendar.
advent.exampleZh: 這款降臨節日曆的每扇門後面都藏著一塊巧克力。

adventurous.definition: (of a person) Inclined to adventure; willing to incur risks; prone to embark in hazardous enterprise; rashly daring.

employee.definition: An individual who provides labor to a company or another person.
employee.example: One way to encourage your employees to work harder is by giving them incentives.
employee.exampleZh: 鼓勵員工更加努力工作的一種方法是給予他們獎勵。

endlessly.definition: In an endless manner; continuously without limit.
endlessly.exampleZh: 反社會者無止盡地說謊。

embassy.definition: An organization or group of officials who permanently represent a sovereign state in a second sovereign state or with respect to an international organization such as the United Nations.
embassy.example: The American embassy to France is located in Paris.
embassy.exampleZh: 美國駐法國大使館位於巴黎。

endorse.definition: To support, to back, to give one's approval to, especially officially or by signature.
endorse.exampleZh: 我全心全意地支持這個決定。

energy.definition: The impetus behind all motion and all activity.

enduring.definition: Long-lasting without significant alteration; continuing through time in the same relative state.

endurance.definition: The measure of a person's stamina or persistence.
endurance.example: He has great endurance: he ran a marathon and then cycled home.
endurance.exampleZh: 他有很強的耐力：他跑了一場馬拉松，然後騎自行車回家。

engagement.definition: An appointment, especially to speak or perform.
engagement.example: The lecturer has three speaking engagements this week.

enforce.definition: To keep up, impose or bring into effect something, not necessarily by force.
enforce.example: The police are there to enforce the law.

engulf.example: Desperation engulfed her after her daughter's death.
engulf.exampleZh: 女兒死後，絕望籠罩著她。

enterprise.definition: A company, business, organization, or other purposeful endeavor.
enterprise.example: A micro-enterprise is defined as a business having 5 or fewer employees and a low seed capital.
enterprise.exampleZh: 微型企業被定義為擁有 5 名或更少員工且原始資本較低的企業。

enhance.definition: To be raised up; to grow larger.
enhance.example: A debt enhances rapidly by compound interest.
enhance.exampleZh: 債務透過複利迅速增加。

entrepreneur.definition: A person who organizes and operates a business venture and assumes much of the associated risk.

entitle.definition: To give power or authority (to do something).
entitle.example: A passport entitles the bearer to travel to other countries.
entitle.exampleZh: 護照使持有人有權前往其他國家。

environmentally.definition: In a manner affecting the environment.

environment.definition: The software and/or hardware existing on any particular computer system.
environment.example: That program uses the Microsoft Windows environment.
environment.exampleZh: 該程式使用 Microsoft Windows 環境。

envy.definition: To feel displeasure or hatred towards (someone) for their good fortune or possessions.

equip.definition: To supply with something necessary in order to carry out a specific action or task; to provide with (e.g. weapons, provisions, munitions, rigging)
equip.exampleZh: 博爾迪購買了紋身設備。

equal.definition: A person or thing of equal status to others.

equation.definition: The act or process of equating two or more things, or the state of those things being equal (that is, identical).
equation.example: We need to bring the balance of power into equation
equation.exampleZh: 我們需要將力量平衡納入方程式

equity.definition: Fairness, impartiality, or justice as determined in light of "natural law" or "natural right".
equity.example: They insist on equity in return for their investments.
equity.exampleZh: 他們堅持以股權作為投資報酬。

eruption.definition: A sudden release of pressure or tension.
eruption.example: There was an eruption of joy at the final whistle.
eruption.exampleZh: 終場哨聲響起時，全場爆出喜悅。

erect.definition: (of body parts) Rigid, firm; standing out perpendicularly, especially as the result of stimulation.
erect.example: The penis should be fully erect before commencing copulation.
erect.exampleZh: 開始交配前陰莖應完全勃起。

erupt.definition: To eject something violently (such as lava or water, as from a volcano or geyser).
erupt.example: The volcano erupted, spewing lava across a wide area.
erupt.exampleZh: 火山爆發，熔岩噴灑到大片地區。

essentially.definition: In an essential manner; in essence

essayist.definition: One who composes essays; a writer of short compositions.
essayist.example: She was an essayist whose frequent contributions to the editorial pages of major newspapers had a loyal following.
essayist.exampleZh: 她是一位散文家，經常為主要報紙的社論版撰稿，擁有一群忠實的追隨者。

unconventional.definition: Not adhering to convention or accepted standards
unconventional.exampleZh: 她的男友是個完全不照常理出牌的人。

purity.definition: The state or degree of being pure.
purity.example: Russian vodka is known for its smoothness and purity.
purity.exampleZh: 俄羅斯伏特加以其柔滑和純淨而聞名。

effortless.exampleZh: 湯姆讓它看起來毫不費力。

induct.definition: To bring in as a member; to make a part of.
induct.example: Franklin was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1987, the first female inductee  [...]
induct.exampleZh: 富蘭克林於 1987 年入選搖滾名人堂，成為第一位女性入選者 [...]

refreshingly.example: a refreshingly original film in which the villain defeated the hero
refreshingly.exampleZh: 一部令人耳目一新的原創電影，其中反派擊敗了英雄

geological.definition: Of, or relating to geology or a geologic time scale.

house.definition: A structure built or serving as an abode of human beings.
house.example: This is my house and my family's ancestral home.
house.exampleZh: 這是我的家，也是我家的祖屋。

stock.definition: To have on hand for sale.
stock.example: The store stocks all kinds of dried vegetables.

fossil.definition: The mineralized remains of an animal or plant.

gem.definition: Any precious or highly valued thing or person.

vertical.definition: Standing, pointing, or moving straight up or down; along the direction of a plumb line; perpendicular to something horizontal.
vertical.example: Vertical farms could reduce water usage.
vertical.exampleZh: 垂直農場可以減少用水量。

specimen.definition: An individual instance that represents a class; an example.
specimen.example: early specimens of the art of Picasso
specimen.exampleZh: 畢卡索藝術的早期標本

enormously.definition: Extremely, greatly: to an enormous degree.
enormously.example: Gloria is enormously pleased with our progress.
enormously.exampleZh: 格洛麗亞對我們的進展非常滿意。

explanatory.definition: Intended to serve as an explanation.
explanatory.example: Below the diagram is an explanatory text.

tomb.definition: A small building (or "vault") for the remains of the dead, with walls, a roof, and (if it is to be used for more than one corpse) a door. It may be partly or wholly in the ground (except for its entrance) in a cemetery, or it may be inside a church proper or in its crypt. Single tombs may be permanently sealed; those for families (or other groups) have doors for access whenever needed.

hanging.example: The lights hung from the ceiling.

ordinarily.definition: Usually or as a general rule; commonly.
ordinarily.example: Ordinarily I go to the match on a Saturday.
ordinarily.exampleZh: 通常我會在星期六去看比賽。

knowledgeable.definition: Having knowledge, especially of a particular subject.

cleanly.definition: Being habitually clean, practising good hygiene.

formulate.definition: To reduce to, or express in, a formula; to put in a clear and definite form of statement or expression.
formulate.example: I cannot coherently formulate words without coffee.
formulate.exampleZh: 沒有咖啡我就無法連貫地表達單字。

endorsement.definition: The act or quality of endorsing
endorsement.example: Companies sometimes pay millions for product endorsement by celebrities.
endorsement.exampleZh: 公司有時會支付數百萬美元來請名人代言產品。

resin.definition: A viscous hydrocarbon secretion of many plants, particularly coniferous trees.

sacrifice.definition: The destruction or surrender of anything for the sake of something else; the devotion of something desirable to something higher, or to a calling deemed more pressing.
sacrifice.example: the sacrifice of one's spare time in order to volunteer
sacrifice.exampleZh: 為了做志工而犧牲自己的業餘時間

stretching.definition: The act by which something is stretched.

claustrophobia.definition: The fear of closed, tight places.
claustrophobia.example: Long elevator rides can be uncomfortable for those who suffer from claustrophobia.
claustrophobia.exampleZh: 對於患有幽閉恐懼症的人來說，長時間搭乘電梯可能會感到不舒服。

claustrophobic.definition: Suffering from claustrophobia; being scared of being enclosed in a confined space.

continuity.definition: Lack of interruption or disconnection; the quality of being continuous in space or time.
continuity.example: Considerable continuity of attention is needed to read German philosophy.
continuity.exampleZh: 閱讀德國哲學需要相當連續的注意力。

headstrong.definition: Determined to do as one pleases, and not as others want.
headstrong.example: He was in that headstrong teenage phase when he felt like he knew everything.
headstrong.exampleZh: 他正處於那個任性的青少年階段，當時他覺得自己什麼都知道。

tactful.definition: Possessing tact; able to deal with people in a sensitive manner.

tact.definition: Sensitive mental touch; special skill or faculty; keen perception or discernment; ready power of appreciating and doing what is required by circumstances; the ability to say the right thing.
tact.example: By the use of tact, she was able to calm her jealous husband.
tact.exampleZh: 她用機智安撫了嫉妒的丈夫。

plummet.definition: To drop swiftly, in a direct manner; to fall quickly.
plummet.example: After its ascent, the arrow plummeted to earth.
plummet.exampleZh: 箭上升後，垂直落向地面。

muscular.definition: Of, relating to, or connected with muscles.
muscular.exampleZh: 卡爾曼是個肌肉發達的怪物。

dislodge.definition: To remove or force out from a position or dwelling previously occupied.
dislodge.example: The blow of the hammer was enough to dislodge the loose brick.
dislodge.exampleZh: 錘子的敲擊足以將鬆動的磚塊移開。

prophetic.definition: Of, or relating to a prophecy or a prophet

paywall.definition: A feature of a website, application or service that only allows access to certain content or functions upon payment.
paywall.example: I can't read the article because of the paywall.
paywall.exampleZh: 由於付費專區，我無法閱讀這篇文章。

invoice.definition: The lot or set of goods as shipped or received.
invoice.example: The merchant receives a large invoice of goods.
invoice.exampleZh: 商家收到大額貨物發票。

firewall.definition: A fireproof barrier used to prevent the spread of fire between or through buildings, structures, electrical substation transformers, or within an aircraft or vehicle.
firewall.example: I need to configure this server with an appropriate firewall system.
firewall.exampleZh: 我需要使用適當的防火牆系統來設定該伺服器。

demolition.definition: The process of demolishing or destroying buildings or other structures.
demolition.example: This was the first time that Tom had ever seen a demolition derby.
demolition.exampleZh: 這是湯姆第一次看到拆除德比。

gig.definition: A performing engagement by a musical group; or, generally, any job or role, especially for a musician or performer.
gig.example: Hey, when are we gonna get that hotel gig again?
gig.exampleZh: 嘿，我們什麼時候才能再次參加酒店表演？

socialization.definition: The process of learning how to live in a way acceptable to one's own society, said especially about children.
socialization.example: Socialization skills are important things to learn in kindergarten.
socialization.exampleZh: 社交技能是幼兒園學習的重要內容。

antiquity.definition: Ancient times; faraway history; former ages
antiquity.example: Cicero was an eloquent orator of antiquity.
antiquity.exampleZh: 西塞羅是古代一位雄辯的演說家。

demo.example: The professor prepared a demo to help her class understand the topic.
demo.exampleZh: 教授準備了一個演示來幫助她的班級理解這個主題。

antiquated.example: The word "antiquated" is commonly used in formal English.

restoration.definition: The process of bringing an object back to its original state; the process of restoring something.
restoration.example: The restoration of this medieval church involved undoing all the Victorian modifications.
restoration.exampleZh: 這座中世紀教堂的修復涉及撤銷所有維多利亞時代的修改。

restorative.example: After a long day working in the fields Clarence took comfort in a restorative pint of beer.
restorative.exampleZh: 在田裡勞動了一整天后，克拉倫斯喝了一品脫啤酒以恢復體力。

coupled.definition: To join (two things) together, or (one thing) to (another).
coupled.example: I've coupled our system to theirs.
coupled.exampleZh: 我已將我們的系統與他們的系統聯繫起來。

till.definition: Until; to, up to; as late as (a given time).
till.example: I have to work till eight o'clock tonight.
till.exampleZh: 我今晚必須工作到八點。

itinerary.definition: A written schedule of activities for a vacation or road trip.
itinerary.exampleZh: 傑諾斯向瑪莎展示了行程。

rotate.example: He rotated in his chair to face me.
rotate.exampleZh: 他在椅子上旋轉面對我。

charter.definition: To grant or establish a charter.
charter.example: The prime minister signed the charter.

resilience.definition: The mental ability to recover quickly from depression, illness or misfortune.
resilience.exampleZh: 她的韌性是無與倫比的。

terrain.definition: An area of land or the particular features of it.
terrain.example: The race will be run over a variety of terrain, including grass and sand.
terrain.exampleZh: 比賽將在各種地形上進行，包括草地和沙地。

resilient.definition: (of objects or substances) Returning quickly to original shape after force is applied; elastic.

enterprising.definition: Displaying bravery and daring in attempting some task.
enterprising.example: Tom is an enterprising young man.
enterprising.exampleZh: 湯姆是個有進取心的年輕人。

integral.definition: Constituting a whole together with other parts or factors; not omittable or removable
integral.exampleZh: 我在溝通上優先考慮誠信。

canoe.definition: A small long and narrow boat, propelled by one or more people (depending on the size of canoe), using single-bladed paddles. The paddlers face in the direction of travel, in either a seated position, or kneeling on the bottom of the boat. Canoes are open on top, and pointed at both ends.

magnetic.definition: Of, relating to, operating by, or caused by magnetism.

credible.exampleZh: 他的故事聽起來很可信。

credibly.example: A suitor can credibly signal his love for a woman by offering her an expensive and extravagant gift.
credibly.exampleZh: 追求者可以透過向一位女性贈送昂貴而奢侈的禮物來可信地表達他對她的愛。

credibility.definition: Reputation impacting one's ability to be believed.
credibility.example: The word "credibility" is commonly used in formal English.
credibility.exampleZh: 「可信度」一詞在正式英語中常用。

prissy.definition: Excessively prim, proper, particular or fussy.
prissy.example: I don't like that prissy face of yours.
prissy.exampleZh: 我不喜歡你那張拘謹的臉。

stultify.definition: To prove to be of unsound mind or demonstrate someone's incompetence.

vicinity.definition: Proximity; the state of being near.
vicinity.example: There was a crackling sound in the vicinity of my right ear.
vicinity.exampleZh: 右耳附近傳來噼裡啪啦的聲音。

austere.definition: Grim or severe in manner or appearance
austere.example: The headmistress was an austere old woman.
austere.exampleZh: 校長是一位認真的老婦人。

calamitous.example: The city was struck by a calamitous cyclone.
calamitous.exampleZh: 這座城市遭受了一場災難性的龍捲風襲擊。

calamity.definition: An event resulting in great loss.
calamity.example: The anticipation is always worse than the calamity.

idiomatic.definition: Pertaining or conforming to the natural mode of expression of a language.
idiomatic.example: Idiomatic expressions can seldom be translated word for word.
idiomatic.exampleZh: 慣用表達很少可以逐字翻譯。

idiosyncratic.definition: Peculiar to a specific individual; eccentric.
idiosyncratic.example: The idiosyncratic watch smith watched me from behind the window.
idiosyncratic.exampleZh: 那個古怪的鐘錶匠從窗戶後面看著我。

austerity.definition: Severity of manners or life; extreme rigor or strictness; harsh discipline.
austerity.example: Parliament approved the austerity measures by a narrow majority.
austerity.exampleZh: 議會以微弱多數批准了緊縮措施。

idiosyncrasy.definition: A peculiarity that serves to distinguish or identify.
idiosyncrasy.example: He mastered the idiosyncrasies of English spelling and speech.
idiosyncrasy.exampleZh: 他掌握了英語拼字和演講的特質。

unmediated.example: The main feature of the process is the presence of unmediated and mutual political will.
unmediated.exampleZh: 這一進程的主要特徵是存在未經調解的共同政治意願。

kinetic.example: That kinetic typography video you made was awesome, Jim!
kinetic.exampleZh: 你製作的動態排版影片太棒了，吉姆！

mediate.definition: To resolve differences, or to bring about a settlement, between conflicting parties.
mediate.example: He always tends to mediate in arguments between friends.
mediate.exampleZh: 他總是喜歡調解朋友之間的爭吵。

mediation.definition: Negotiation to resolve differences conducted by some impartial party.
mediation.example: The RAID negotiator specializes in mediation during high-stakes critical events.
mediation.exampleZh: RAID 談判員專門從事高風險關鍵事件期間的調解。

exorcism.definition: The ritual act of driving out evil spirits from persons, places or things who are possessed by them.
exorcism.example: Exorcism is practiced in Algeria because we see evil everywhere.
exorcism.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞實行驅魔，因為我們到處都看到邪惡。

figuratively.example: The word "figuratively" is commonly used in formal English.
figuratively.exampleZh: 「比喻地」一詞在正式英語中常用。

mediocrity.definition: The quality of being intermediate between two extremes; a mean.

exorcist.definition: A person, especially a priest, who practices exorcism.
exorcist.example: He departed with the speed of a demon dismissed by the exorcist.
exorcist.exampleZh: 他離開的速度就像被驅魔人驅逐的惡魔一樣。

curate.example: The museum is known to curate eccentric pieces.
curate.exampleZh: 博物館以收藏古怪的作品而聞名。

antithetical.definition: Pertaining to antithesis, or opposition of words and sentiments; containing, or of the nature of, antithesis; contrasted.
antithetical.example: His wrong-headed beliefs are antithetical to everything we stand for as a community.
antithetical.exampleZh: 他的錯誤信念與我們作為一個社群所代表的一切背道而馳。

curator.definition: A person who manages, administers or organizes a collection, either independently or employed by a museum, library, archive or zoo.
curator.example: Elena is the curator of a modern art gallery.
curator.exampleZh: 埃琳娜是現代藝術畫廊的館長。

antithesis.definition: A proposition that is the diametric opposite of some other proposition.
antithesis.example: A moral monopoly is the antithesis of a marketplace of ideas.
antithesis.exampleZh: 道德壟斷是思想市場的對立面。

parameter.definition: An input variable of a procedure definition, that gets an actual value (argument) at execution time (formal parameter).
parameter.example: Roughly, a tuple of arguments could be thought of as a vector, whereas a tuple of parameters could be thought of as a covector (i.e., linear functional). When a function is called, a parameter tuple becomes "bound" to an argument tuple, allowing the function instance itself to be computed to yield a return value. This would be roughly analogous to applying a covector to a vector (by taking their dot product (or, rather, matrix-product of row vector and column vector)) to obtain a scalar.
parameter.exampleZh: 粗略地說，參數元組可以被視為向量，而參數元組可以被視為協向量（即線性函數）。當呼叫函數時，參數元組「綁定」到參數元組，從而允許計算函數實例本身以產生返回值。這大致類似於將協向量應用於向量（透過取它們的點積（或更確切地說，行向量和列向量的矩陣積））來獲得標量。

detract.definition: To take away; to withdraw or remove.
detract.example: I'm sorry; I didn't mean to detract from what you were saying.
detract.exampleZh: 對不起;我無意貶低你所說的話。

interrogation.definition: The act of interrogating or questioning; examination by questions; inquiry.

interrogate.definition: To question or quiz, especially in a thorough and/or aggressive manner
interrogate.example: The police interrogated the suspect at some length before they let him go.
interrogate.exampleZh: 警方對嫌疑人進行了一番訊問，然後才將其釋放。

interrogative.example: He took on an interrogative tone of voice.

perverse.definition: Turned aside; hence, specifically, turned away from the (morally) right; willfully erring; wicked; perverted.
perverse.example: Don't think I'm perverse, but I've seen several condoms in her purse.
perverse.exampleZh: 別以為我很乖僻，但我在她的錢包裡看到了好幾個保險套。

perversity.example: Don't think I'm perverse, but I've seen several condoms in her purse.
perversity.exampleZh: 別以為我很乖僻，但我在她的錢包裡看到了好幾個保險套。

adverse.definition: Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.

affectionate.definition: (of a person) Having affection or warm regard; loving; fond.
affectionate.example: She eulogised her always warm and affectionate brother.
affectionate.exampleZh: 她稱讚她總是溫暖而深情的哥哥。

adversity.definition: The state of adverse conditions; state of misfortune or calamity.

affordable.definition: Able to be afforded; inexpensive or reasonably priced.
affordable.example: an affordable vacation in the local area
affordable.exampleZh: 在當地度過一個經濟實惠的假期

ageless.definition: (relative to past) Having existed for so great a period of time that its longevity cannot be expressed.
ageless.example: The ageless pyramids stood prominently against the sunset.
ageless.exampleZh: 永恆的金字塔在夕陽的襯托下格外顯眼。

aim.definition: To point or direct a missile, or a weapon which propels as missile, towards an object or spot with the intent of hitting it
aim.example: He aimed at the target, but the arrow flew straight over it.
aim.exampleZh: 他瞄準了目標，但箭卻筆直地飛過目標。

aircraft.definition: A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift
aircraft.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞擁有先進的戰鬥機。

alcoholism.definition: A chronic disease caused by compulsive and uncontrollable consumption of alcoholic beverages, leading to addiction and deterioration in health and social functioning.

aircrew.definition: A group of two or more trained individuals, formed as a team that operates an aircraft.
aircrew.example: The aircrew ran to the bomber.

alert.definition: A state of readiness for potential combat.

allegation.definition: An assertion, especially an accusation, not necessarily based on facts.
allegation.example: She put forth several allegations regarding her partner in hopes of discrediting his actions.
allegation.exampleZh: 她對她的伴侶提出了幾項指控，希望抹黑他的行為。

alliance.example: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries
alliance.exampleZh: 婚姻聯盟；教會與國家或兩國之間的聯盟

ally.definition: A person, group, or state (etc) which is associated with another for a common cause; one united to another by treaty or common purpose; a confederate.
ally.example: the two countries were allies in WWI
ally.exampleZh: 兩國在第一次世界大戰中是盟友

alongside.definition: Together with or at the same time.
alongside.example: The nurse worked alongside the doctor.

altar.definition: A table or similar flat-topped structure used for religious rites.
altar.example: The altar of media believers is television.
altar.exampleZh: 媒體信徒的祭壇是電視。

alphabetical.definition: According to the sequence of the letters of the alphabet.
alphabetical.example: All names were placed into an alphabetical list.
alphabetical.exampleZh: 所有名字都按字母順序排列在列表中。

alter.definition: To change the form or structure of.
alter.exampleZh: 沒有什麼可以改變過去。

alternate.definition: To happen, succeed, or act by turns; to follow reciprocally in place or time; followed by with.
alternate.example: The flood and ebb tides alternate with each other.

altitude.definition: The absolute height of a location, usually measured from sea level.
altitude.example: As the altitude increases, the temperature gets lower, so remember to bring warm clothes to the mountains.
altitude.exampleZh: 隨著海拔的升高，氣溫逐漸降低，所以上山記得帶保暖的衣服。

alternatively.exampleZh: 他探索了替代解決方案。

amaze.definition: To fill with wonder and surprise; to astonish, astound, surprise or perplex.
amaze.example: He was amazed when he found that the girl was a robot.
amaze.exampleZh: 當他發現這個女孩是機器人時，他感到很驚訝。

amazingly.definition: In an amazing manner; in a way that causes amazement; wonderfully.
amazingly.example: That violin solo was played amazingly.
amazingly.exampleZh: 那段小提琴獨奏演奏得令人驚艷。

amazement.definition: The condition of being amazed; overwhelming wonder, as from surprise, sudden fear, horror, or admiration; astonishment.
amazement.example: The word "amazement" is commonly used in formal English.

ambassador.definition: A corporate representative, often the public face of the company.
ambassador.example: As front hall porter, you are an ambassador for the hotel.
ambassador.exampleZh: 身為前廳行李員，您是飯店的大使。

ambiguity.definition: Something, particularly words and sentences, that is open to more than one interpretation, explanation or meaning, if that meaning etc cannot be determined from its context.
ambiguity.example: His speech was made with such great ambiguity that neither supporter nor opponent could be certain of his true position.
ambiguity.exampleZh: 他的演講非常含糊，無論是支持者還是反對者都無法確定他的真實立場。

ambiguous.example: The politician was criticized for his ambiguous statements and lack of precision.
ambiguous.exampleZh: 這位政客因其言論含糊和缺乏精確性而受到批評。

ammonia.definition: A gaseous compound of hydrogen and nitrogen, NH3, with a pungent smell and taste.
ammonia.example: The ammonia molecule is composed of four atoms.
ammonia.exampleZh: 氨分子由四個原子組成。

ample.definition: Large; great in size, extent, capacity, or bulk; for example spacious, roomy or widely extended.

amongst.definition: Denotes a mingling or intermixing with distinct or separable objects. See usage note at amidst.
amongst.example: Janek feels very at ease amongst his friends, but gets incredibly nervous when meeting new people.
amongst.exampleZh: 賈內克在朋友中感到非常自在，但在結識新朋友時卻變得異常緊張。

amplifier.definition: Anything that amplifies, or makes something larger or more intense.
amplifier.example: The guitar and amplifier are sold separately.
amplifier.exampleZh: 吉他和擴音器單獨販售。

amplify.definition: To render larger, more extended, or more intense.

analogy.definition: A relationship of resemblance or equivalence between two situations, people, or objects, especially when used as a basis for explanation or extrapolation.
analogy.exampleZh: 這個類比基本上是正確的。

amuse.definition: To entertain or occupy in a pleasant manner; to stir with pleasing emotions.
amuse.example: I watch these movies because they amuse me.
amuse.exampleZh: 我看這些電影是因為它們讓我開心。

analyst.example: Yanni is an Algerian financial analyst.
analyst.exampleZh: 雅尼是阿爾及利亞的金融分析師。

anchor.definition: A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.
anchor.exampleZh: 加百列聽到錨落下的聲音。

anchorage.definition: That into which something is anchored or fastened.
anchorage.example: the anchorages of the Brooklyn Bridge

anchorman.definition: The main host of a television or radio program, particularly one relating to the broadcast of news.
anchorman.example: The anchorman had an audience with the king.

anguish.definition: Extreme pain, either of body or mind; excruciating distress.
anguish.example: Yanni could clearly hear the anguish in Rima's voice.
anguish.exampleZh: 雅尼清楚地聽出了麗瑪聲音中的痛苦。

anchorperson.definition: The primary reporter on a television news broadcast.
anchorperson.example: The word "anchorperson" is commonly used in formal English.
anchorperson.exampleZh: 「錨人」一詞在正式英語中常用。

anonymous.definition: Without any name acknowledged of a person responsible

animate.definition: To impart motion or the appearance of motion to.
animate.example: If we animate the model, we can see the complexity of the action.
animate.exampleZh: 如果我們為模型製作動畫，我們可以看到動作的複雜性。

antibacterial.example: This drug has an antibacterial effect.

antiaircraft.example: The word "antiaircraft" is commonly used in formal English.

anticancer.definition: That is used to treat cancer.
anticancer.example: The word "anticancer" is commonly used in formal English.
anticancer.exampleZh: 「抗癌」一詞在正式英語中常用。

anticipate.definition: To take up or introduce (something) prematurely.
anticipate.example: The advocate plans to anticipate a part of her argument.
anticipate.exampleZh: 辯護人計劃預測她的論點的一部分。

anticipation.definition: The act of anticipating, taking up, placing, or considering something beforehand, or before the proper time in natural order.
anticipation.example: Often the anticipation of a shot is worse than the pain of the stick.
anticipation.exampleZh: 通常，射擊的預期比棍子的疼痛更糟糕。

antivirus.definition: A piece of software that is used to detect, delete and or neutralize computer-based viruses.
antivirus.example: Bill Gates wrote viruses and sold antivirus software.
antivirus.exampleZh: 比爾蓋茲編寫病毒並銷售防毒軟體。

antistatic.definition: Preventing the buildup of static electricity.
antistatic.example: The word "antistatic" is commonly used in formal English.
antistatic.exampleZh: 「抗靜電」一詞在正式英語中常用。

appeal.example: I appeal to all of you to help the orphans.

apostrophe.definition: The text character ’, which serves as a punctuation mark in various languages and as a diacritical mark in certain rare contexts.
apostrophe.example: I changed the apostrophe to the straight one that we use.
apostrophe.exampleZh: 我將撇號更改為我們使用的直撇號。

applicant.definition: One who applies for something; one who makes a request; a petitioner.
applicant.example: Our job advertisement attracted seven applicants.
applicant.exampleZh: 我們的招募廣告吸引了七位應徵者。

appropriately.definition: In an appropriate manner; properly; suitably.

approach.definition: To draw near, in a figurative sense; to make advances; to approximate.
approach.example: as he approaches to the character of the ablest statesman.
approach.exampleZh: 當他接近最有能力的政治家的性格。

arch.definition: Any place covered by an arch; an archway.
arch.example: to pass into the arch of a bridge

approximate.definition: Nearing correctness; nearly exact; not perfectly accurate.
approximate.example: NASA's Genesis spacecraft has on board an ion monitor to record the speed, density, temperature and approximate composition of the solar wind ions.
approximate.exampleZh: 美國太空總署的創世紀太空船上裝有離子監視器，用於記錄太陽風離子的速度、密度、溫度和大致成分。

arena.definition: An enclosed area, often outdoor, for the presentation of sporting events (sports arena) or other spectacular events; earthen area, often oval, specifically for rodeos (North America) or circular area for bullfights (especially Hispanic America).
arena.example: A large crowd filled the seats of the arena.
arena.exampleZh: 競技場的座位上擠滿了一大群人。

arctic.definition: (now only in compounds) Pertaining to the celestial north pole, or to the pole star.
arctic.example: She has finally reached the Arctic.

aristocracy.definition: The nobility, or the hereditary ruling class.
aristocracy.example: I did everything possible to appear as a member of the aristocracy.
aristocracy.exampleZh: 我千方百計地以貴族的身份出現。

armful.definition: The amount an arm or arms can hold.
armful.example: The word "armful" is commonly used in formal English.

arouse.example: The new building proposals in the village are arousing unneeded discomfort.
arouse.exampleZh: 村裡的新建築提案引起了不必要的不​​適。

artery.definition: An efferent blood vessel from the heart, conveying blood away from the heart regardless of oxygenation status; see pulmonary artery.
artery.example: I've got a bad heart: coronary artery disease.
artery.exampleZh: 我的心臟不好：冠狀動脈疾病。

arrogant.definition: Having excessive pride in oneself, often with contempt or disrespect for others.

articulate.definition: An animal of the subkingdom Articulata.

artificially.example: The actor spoke his lines artificially, with too much intonation.
artificially.exampleZh: 演員說台詞時很做作，語調太多。

ash.definition: The solid remains of a fire.
ash.example: Ash from a fireplace can restore minerals to your garden's soil.
ash.exampleZh: 壁爐中的灰燼可以將礦物質還原到花園的土壤中。

aspiration.definition: The act of aspiring or ardently desiring; an ardent wish or desire, chiefly after what is elevated or spiritual (with common adjunct adpositions being to and of).
aspiration.example: Morgan has an aspiration of winning the game.
aspiration.exampleZh: 摩根渴望贏得這場比賽。

artistry.example: Their fast talking is a smokescreen for their con artistry.
artistry.exampleZh: 他們的語速很快就只是他們騙術的煙幕彈。

aspire.definition: To have a strong desire or ambition to achieve something.
aspire.example: He aspires to become a successful doctor.
aspire.exampleZh: 他渴望成為一名成功的醫生。

assault.definition: A violent onset or attack with physical means, for example blows, weapons, etc.
assault.example: to make assault upon a man, a house, or a town
assault.exampleZh: 攻擊一個人、一棟房屋或一座城鎮

assembly.definition: A set of pieces that work together in unison as a mechanism or device.
assembly.example: In order to change the bearing, you must first remove the gearbox assembly.
assembly.exampleZh: 為了更換軸承，必須先拆下變速箱總成。

assert.definition: To declare with assurance or plainly and strongly; to state positively.
assert.example: He would often assert that there was life on other planets.
assert.exampleZh: 他經常斷言其他行星上有生命。

assessment.definition: The act of assessing or an amount (of tax, levy or duty etc) assessed.

assess.definition: To determine, estimate or judge the value of; to evaluate

assurance.exampleZh: 他的保證令人難以忍受

assumption.definition: The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.
assumption.example: His assumption of secretarial duties was timely.
assumption.exampleZh: 他擔任秘書職務是及時的。

assure.definition: (followed by that or of) To give (someone) confidence in the trustworthiness of (something).
assure.example: He assured of his commitment to her happiness.
assure.exampleZh: 他向她保證他會致力於讓她幸福。

astonishment.definition: The feeling or experience of being astonished; great surprise.
astonishment.example: The class looked on in astonishment as their teacher proceeded to tear the pages out of the textbook.
astonishment.exampleZh: 當老師開始把課本上的書頁撕下來時，全班都驚訝地看著。

astronomy.definition: The study of the physical universe beyond the Earth's atmosphere, including the process of mapping locations and properties of the matter and radiation in the universe.
astronomy.exampleZh: 馬蒂諾對天文學很有興趣。

athletics.definition: (especially Britain) A group of sporting activities including track and field, road running, cross country and racewalking.

attached.example: I'm very attached to my pets.

attainment.definition: The act of attaining; the act of arriving at or reaching; the act of obtaining by exertion or effort.
attainment.example: The word "attainment" is commonly used in formal English.

attainable.definition: Able to be accomplished, achieved, or obtained.
attainable.exampleZh: 利比亞的和平是可以實現的。

attentive.definition: Paying attention; noticing, watching, listening, or attending closely.
attentive.example: She is an attentive listener, but does not like to talk much.
attentive.exampleZh: 她是個細心的傾聽者，但不喜歡多說話。

attendance.definition: The state of attending; presence or waiting upon.
attendance.example: Attendance at the meeting is required.

attractiveness.definition: The state of being attractive or engaging.
attractiveness.example: The word "attractiveness" is commonly used in formal English.
attractiveness.exampleZh: 「吸引力」一詞在正式英語中常用。

atypical.definition: Not conforming to the normal type.
atypical.example: Hutchinson's freckle is a lentigo composed of atypical melanocytes.
atypical.exampleZh: 哈欽森雀斑是一種由非典型黑色素細胞組成的雀斑。

attribute.definition: A characteristic or quality of a thing.
attribute.example: His finest attribute is his kindness.
attribute.exampleZh: 他最好的品質就是他的善良。

aubergine.definition: An Asian plant, Solanum melongena, cultivated for its edible purple, green, or white ovoid fruit
aubergine.example: Aubergine and cheese makes a great vegetarian meal.
aubergine.exampleZh: 茄子和起司是一頓美味的素食餐。

audition.definition: A performance, by an aspiring performer, to demonstrate suitability or talent.
audition.example: I've been to five auditions this week.
audition.exampleZh: 這週我參加了五次試鏡。

audit.definition: An independent review and examination of records and activities to assess the adequacy of system controls, to ensure compliance with established policies and operational procedures, and to recommend necessary changes in controls, policies, or procedures

authoritative.definition: Arising or originating from a figure of authority
authoritative.example: The authoritative rules in this school come not from the headmaster but from the aged matron.
authoritative.exampleZh: 這所學校的權威規則不是來自校長，而是來自年邁的女校長。

availability.example: What is your availability this week?

awe.definition: A feeling of fear and reverence.
awe.example: Gabriel observed Rima with much awe.
awe.exampleZh: 加百列滿懷敬畏地觀察著莉瑪。

await.example: I await your reply to my letter.
await.exampleZh: 我等待你對我的信的答覆。

axis.definition: An imaginary line around which an object spins (an axis of rotation) or is symmetrically arranged (an axis of symmetry).
axis.example: The Earth rotates once a day on its axis
axis.exampleZh: 地球每天繞地軸自轉一周

backup.example: If the goalkeeper is injured, we have a backup.
backup.exampleZh: 如果守門員受傷了，我們還有替補。

backyard.definition: A yard to the rear of a house or similar residence.

bad-tempered.definition: Of or pertaining to bad temper; showing anger easily or customarily.
bad-tempered.example: His bad-tempered outburst caught his friends by surprise.
bad-tempered.exampleZh: 他的暴躁脾氣讓他的朋友們大吃一驚。

badge.definition: A distinctive mark, token, sign, emblem or cognizance, worn on one's clothing, as an insignia of some rank, or of the membership of an organization.
badge.example: the badge of a society; the badge of a policeman
badge.exampleZh: 社團的徽章；警察的徽章

balance.definition: To hold (an object or objects) precariously; to support on a narrow base, so as to keep from falling.
balance.example: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.
balance.exampleZh: 我把咖啡杯放在膝蓋上。

balanced.definition: Containing elements in appropriate proportion; proportionately weighted on all dimensions and therefore unlikely to tip over.
balanced.example: He believed he rarely got sick because of his balanced diet.
balanced.exampleZh: 他相信，由於飲食均衡，他很少生病。

ballad.definition: A kind of narrative poem, adapted for recitation or singing; especially, a sentimental or romantic poem in short stanzas.
ballad.example: The poet composed a ballad praising the heroic exploits of the fallen commander.
ballad.exampleZh: 這位詩人創作了一首歌謠，歌頌陣亡將領的英勇事蹟。

ballot.definition: Originally, a small ball placed in a container to cast a vote; now, by extension, a piece of paper or card used for this purpose, or some other means used to signify a vote.
ballot.example: You can vote by absentee ballot.
ballot.exampleZh: 您可以透過缺席選票進行投票。

banker.definition: One who conducts the business of banking; one who, individually, or as a member of a company, keeps an establishment for the deposit or loan of money, or for traffic in money, bills of exchange, etc.

ban.definition: A public proclamation or edict; a summons by public proclamation. Chiefly, in early use, a summons to arms.
ban.example: Bans is common and ordinary amongst the Feudists, and signifies a proclamation, or any public notice.
ban.exampleZh: 禁令在封建主義者中是常見且普通的，表示公告或任何公告。

bankruptcy.definition: A legally declared or recognized condition of insolvency of a person or organization.

bare.example: The word "bare" is commonly used in formal English.

barbershop.definition: The place of business of a barber; a store where a person (usually a man) can go to get a haircut.
barbershop.example: The barbershop is opposite the bank.

bargain.definition: To make a bargain; to make a deal or contract for the exchange of property or services; to negotiate
bargain.example: They had to bargain for a few minutes to get a decent price for the rug.
bargain.exampleZh: 他們不得不討價還價幾分鐘才能為地毯拿到一個合適的價格。

barley.definition: A cereal of the species Hordeum vulgare, or its grains, often used as food or to make beer and other malted drinks.

bark.definition: To make a short, loud, explosive noise with the vocal organs (said of animals, especially dogs).
bark.example: The neighbour's dog is always barking.

barn.example: Maple Leaf Gardens was a grand old barn.
barn.exampleZh: 楓葉花園是一座宏偉的舊穀倉。

barometer.definition: An instrument for measuring atmospheric pressure.
barometer.example: The retail price index is a barometer of economic activity.
barometer.exampleZh: 零售物價指數是經濟活動的晴雨表。

barrier.exampleZh: 事實證明，語言障礙是無法克服的。

barren.example: I silently wept as my daughter's husband rejected her. What would she do now that she was no longer a maiden but also barren?
barren.exampleZh: 當我女兒的丈夫拒絕她時，我默默地哭泣。現在她不再是處女，而且還不能生育，她該怎麼辦？

bartender.definition: One who tends a bar or pub; a person preparing and serving drinks at a bar.

bathhouse.definition: A building with baths for communal use.
bathhouse.example: Luckily for Neo, there is a weightlifting set inside the bathhouse.
bathhouse.exampleZh: 對尼奧來說幸運的是，浴室裡有一個舉重器材。

battlefield.definition: The area where a land battle is or was fought, which is not necessarily a field.
battlefield.example: 1886 The night of the 16th of May found McPherson's command bivouacked from two to six miles west of the battlefield, along the line of the road to Vicksburg — Ulysses S. Grant, Personal memoirs of U.S. Grant, Chapter 35.
battlefield.exampleZh: 1886 年 5 月 16 日晚上，麥克弗森的指揮部在距離戰場以西 2 到 6 英里的地方紮營，沿著通往維克斯堡的公路——尤利西斯·S·格蘭特，美國格蘭特的個人回憶錄，第 35 章。

beagle.definition: A small short-legged smooth-coated scenthound, often tricolored and sometimes used for hunting hares. Its friendly disposition makes it suitable as a family pet.
beagle.example: Yet I get no repose and I can't even doze without dreaming I'm back on the Beagle.
beagle.exampleZh: 然而我卻得不到休息，我甚至無法打瞌睡而不夢見自己回到了小獵犬號上。

beak.example: What bird has the longest beak?

beam.definition: The maximum width of a vessel (note that a vessel with a beam of 15 foot can also be said to be 15 foot abeam)
beam.example: This ship has more beam than that one.
beam.exampleZh: 這艘船的橫樑比那艘多。

bearing.definition: To endeavour to depress the price of, or prices in.

beat.example: a beat of the heart; the beat of the pulse

beautify.definition: To make beautiful, or to increase the beauty of.
beautify.example: Hundreds of years ago, married Japanese women would blacken their teeth to beautify themselves.
beautify.exampleZh: 數百年前，已婚的日本女性會把牙齒塗黑以美化自己。

bellow.definition: To make a loud, deep, hollow noise like the roar of an angry bull.
bellow.example: Do the oxen bellow when their mangers are full?
bellow.exampleZh: 當牛的馬槽滿了時，它們會吼叫嗎？

belongings.example: A need for belonging seems fundamental to humans.
belongings.exampleZh: 歸屬感的需求似乎是人類的基本需求。

bend.example: There's a sharp bend in the road ahead.
bend.exampleZh: 前面的路有一個急轉彎。

beneficial.definition: Helpful or good to something or someone.
beneficial.example: Recycling and reusing garbage can be beneficial to the environment.
beneficial.exampleZh: 回收和再利用垃圾可能對環境有益。

benefit.definition: To be or to provide a benefit to.

benevolence.example: A certain hallmark of wisdom is the cheerful benevolence it confers.
benevolence.exampleZh: 智慧的一個標誌是它所帶來的令人愉快的仁慈。

benevolent.definition: Having a disposition to do good.
benevolent.example: Chinese and Eastern mythologies describe dragons as benevolent.
benevolent.exampleZh: 中國和東方神話將龍描述為仁慈的。

berry.definition: A small succulent fruit, of any one of many varieties.

bestow.definition: To give; confer; impart gratuitously; present something to someone or something, especially as a gift or honour.
bestow.example: Medals were bestowed on the winning team.

betray.definition: To deliver into the hands of an enemy by treachery or fraud, in violation of trust; to give up treacherously or faithlessly.
betray.exampleZh: 一名軍官背叛了這座城市

bestseller.definition: A book or other product that has sold in large numbers.
bestseller.example: Her book became an international bestseller.
bestseller.exampleZh: 她的書成為國際暢銷書。

bewilder.definition: To confuse, disorientate, or puzzle someone, especially with many different choices.
bewilder.example: All the different possible options may bewilder us.
bewilder.exampleZh: 所有不同的可能選擇可能會讓我們感到困惑。

bewilderment.example: Tom's face clearly showed bewilderment coupled with irritation.
bewilderment.exampleZh: 湯姆的臉上明顯表現出困惑和惱怒。

bid.definition: To issue a command; to tell.

bidding.definition: That which one is bidden to do; a command.

bikini.definition: A brief two-piece bathing suit worn by women, especially one that exposes the midriff and navel.
bikini.example: Rima was walking around in a bikini.
bikini.exampleZh: 裡瑪穿著比基尼四處走動。

billion.example: There were billions of people at the concert.
billion.exampleZh: 音樂會現場有數十億人。

bind.definition: To cohere or stick together in a mass.
bind.example: Just to make the cheese more binding
bind.exampleZh: 只是為了讓起司更有黏合力

bio.example: If you use someone else's drawing as your profile picture, make sure to credit the artist in your bio.
bio.exampleZh: 如果您使用其他人的畫作作為您的個人資料圖片，請務必在您的個人簡介中註明該藝術家。

birdcage.definition: A cage to keep pet or zoological specimen birds in.
birdcage.example: There's a parrot in the birdcage.

biotechnology.definition: The use of living organisms (especially microorganisms) in industrial, agricultural, medical and other technological applications.
biotechnology.example: Biotechnology will bring about a revolution in agriculture.
biotechnology.exampleZh: 生物技術將帶來農業革命。

birthplace.definition: The location where a person was born.
birthplace.exampleZh: 朴茨茅斯是查爾斯·狄更斯的出生地。

bizarre.definition: Strangely unconventional in style or appearance.

blast.definition: The continuous blowing to which one charge of ore or metal is subjected in a furnace
blast.example: many tons of iron were melted at a blast
blast.exampleZh: 許多噸鐵一下子就被熔化了

blaze.definition: Intense, direct light accompanied with heat.
blaze.example: to seek shelter from the blaze of the sun
blaze.exampleZh: 尋求庇護以躲避烈日的炙烤

blade.definition: The sharp cutting edge of a knife, chisel, or other tool, a razor blade/sword.

blend.definition: A mixture of two or more things.
blend.example: Our department has a good blend of experienced workers and young promise.
blend.exampleZh: 我們部門擁有經驗豐富的員工和年輕有為的員工。

blindly.definition: In a blind manner; without sight; sightlessly.
blindly.example: I stumbled blindly through the forest, branches whipping at my face.
blindly.exampleZh: 我盲目地跌跌撞撞地穿過森林，樹枝拍打著我的臉。

blink.definition: To close and reopen both eyes quickly.
blink.example: The loser in the staring game is the person who blinks first.
blink.exampleZh: 凝視遊戲中的失敗者是先眨眼的人。

blindness.definition: The condition of being blind; unable to see.
blindness.example: The word "blindness" is commonly used in formal English.

bloody.example: All that remained of his right hand after the accident was a bloody stump.
bloody.exampleZh: 事故發生後，他的右手只剩下血淋淋的殘肢。

bloodstream.definition: The flow of blood through the circulatory system of an animal
bloodstream.example: The vaccine is not injected into the bloodstream, but into the muscle.
bloodstream.exampleZh: 疫苗不是注射到血液中，而是注射到肌肉中。

blossom.definition: A flower, especially one indicating that a fruit tree is fruiting; a mass of such flowers.
blossom.example: The blossom has come early this year.

blur.definition: To make indistinct or hazy, to obscure or dim.
blur.example: to blur a photograph by moving the camera while taking it
blur.exampleZh: 透過在拍攝時移動相機來模糊照片

blush.definition: To become red in the face (and sometimes experience an associated feeling of warmth), especially due to shyness, shame, excitement, or embarrassment.
blush.example: He wasn't used to this much attention, so he blushed as he saw dozens of pairs of eyes watching him.
blush.exampleZh: 他不太習慣這麼多的關注，看到幾十雙眼睛注視著他，他臉紅了。

blurt.example: Please think about your reply and don't just blurt out the first thing that comes to mind.
blurt.exampleZh: 請考慮一下你的回答，不要只是脫口而出你想到的第一件事。

bolt.definition: A (usually) metal fastener consisting of a cylindrical body that is threaded, with a larger head on one end. It can be inserted into an unthreaded hole up to the head, with a nut then threaded on the other end; a heavy machine screw.
bolt.example: The problem's solution struck him like a bolt from the blue.
bolt.exampleZh: 問題的解決對他來說猶如晴天霹靂。

bombard.definition: A medieval primitive cannon, used chiefly in sieges for throwing heavy stone balls.
bombard.example: Nothing would escape a Chinese bombardment in Taiwan.
bombard.exampleZh: 沒有什麼能逃脫中國對台灣的轟炸。

bombing.definition: The action of dropping bombs from the air.
bombing.example: I was bombing down the road on my motorbike.
bombing.exampleZh: 我騎著摩托車在路上狂奔。

bomber.definition: A military aircraft designed to carry and drop bombs.
bomber.example: My grandfather got seriously injured by a bomber during the war.
bomber.exampleZh: 我的祖父在戰爭期間被轟炸機嚴重炸傷。

bony.definition: Resembling, having the appearance or consistence of, or relating to bone; osseous.
bony.example: Look at him! He's bony, he can barely pick up a bottle of water.
bony.exampleZh: 看看他！他骨瘦如柴，幾乎連一瓶水都拿不起來。

bookish.definition: Fond of reading or studying, especially said of someone lacking social skills as a result.
bookish.example: She's undergone a metamorphosis from a quiet, bookish girl to an outspoken feminist activist.
bookish.exampleZh: 她經歷了從一個安靜、書生氣的女孩到一個直言不諱的女權運動家的蛻變。

booklet.example: The complete booklet is available for free online.
booklet.exampleZh: 完整的小冊子可在線上免費取得。

boost.definition: A push from behind, as to one who is endeavoring to climb.
boost.example: The controversy gave a boost to the author's sales.
boost.exampleZh: 這場爭議促進了作者的銷售。

bookmark.definition: A strip of material used to mark a place in a book.

booth.definition: A small stall for the display and sale of goods.

botany.definition: The scientific study of plants, a branch of biology. Typically those disciplines that involve the whole plant.

bossy.definition: Tending to give orders to others, especially when unwarranted; domineering.

bothered.example: Would it bother you if I smoked?
bothered.exampleZh: 如果我抽煙你會介意嗎？

bottle.definition: To seal (a liquid) into a bottle for later consumption. Also fig.
bottle.example: This plant bottles vast quantities of spring water every day.
bottle.exampleZh: 這家工廠每天都會生產大量的泉水。

bothersome.example: Do you think that bothersome things disappear if you ignore them?
bothersome.exampleZh: 你認為如果你忽略那些煩人的事情，它們就會消失嗎？

bounce.definition: A change of direction of motion after hitting the ground or an obstacle.
bounce.exampleZh: 他們的職業球員得到了反彈！

bourbon.definition: A whiskey distilled from a mixture of grains in which at least 51% is corn, aged in charred, new oak barrels. Made in the United States.
bourbon.example: Mary poured herself another shot of bourbon.
bourbon.exampleZh: 瑪莉又為自己倒了一杯波本威士忌。

bow.definition: To play music on (a stringed) instrument using a bow.
bow.example: The musician bowed his violin expertly.
bow.exampleZh: 音樂家熟練地拉著小提琴。

boxer.definition: A participant (fighter) in a boxing match.
boxer.example: You can tell she's a boxer by looking at her nose.
boxer.exampleZh: 看她的鼻子就知道她是拳擊手。

brag.definition: A boast or boasting; bragging; ostentatious pretence or self-glorification.
brag.example: to brag of one's exploits, courage, or money
brag.exampleZh: 吹噓自己的功績、勇氣或金錢

bracket.definition: A fixture attached to a wall to hold up a shelf.

breakable.definition: Able to break or be broken.
breakable.example: We had to wrap all the breakables before the movers arrived.
breakable.exampleZh: 我們必須在搬運工到達之前把所有易碎物品包起來。

bravery.example: Caesar surpassed all men in bravery.
bravery.exampleZh: 凱撒的勇敢超越了所有人。

breakdown.definition: A failure, particularly mechanical; something that has failed
breakdown.example: We saw a breakdown by the side of the road.
breakdown.exampleZh: 我們在路邊看到了故障。

breathtaking.example: He went to the Grand Canyon and spent a week taking in the breathtaking scenery all around him.
breathtaking.exampleZh: 他去了大峽谷，花了一周的時間欣賞周圍令人驚嘆的風景。

breakup.definition: The act of breaking up; disintegration or division.
breakup.example: David and Amanda announced their breakup.
breakup.exampleZh: 大衛和阿曼達宣布分手。

breed.definition: All animals or plants of the same species or subspecies.

breezy.definition: With a breeze blowing, with a lively wind, pleasantly windy.
breezy.example: Close the window, it's cold and breezy outside.
breezy.exampleZh: 關上窗戶，外頭涼風習習。

brewery.definition: A building where beer is produced.
brewery.example: They couldn't organise a piss-up in a brewery.
brewery.exampleZh: 他們不能在啤酒廠裡組織一場小便。

brilliantly.definition: In a brilliant manner; with brilliance.

broadcast.definition: To transmit a message or signal through radio waves or electronic means.
broadcast.example: The DJ was feeling nervous before his first national broadcast.
broadcast.exampleZh: DJ 在第一次全國廣播前感到緊張。

broadband.definition: A wide band of electromagnetic frequencies
broadband.example: There are big differences in broadband speed and quality from country to country.
broadband.exampleZh: 不同國家的寬頻速度和品質有很大差異。

broadcaster.definition: An organisation that engages in the activity of broadcasting.
broadcaster.exampleZh: NHK 是一家公共廣播公司。

brochure.definition: A booklet of printed informational matter, like a pamphlet, often for promotional purposes.
brochure.exampleZh: 列昂尼德找到了學校的小冊子。

brother-in-law.definition: A male relative of one's generation, separated by one degree of marriage:
brother-in-law.exampleZh: 我的妹夫上週三去世了。

browse.definition: To scan, to casually look through in order to find items of interest, especially without knowledge of what to look for beforehand.

bruise.definition: A purplish mark on the skin due to leakage of blood from capillaries under the surface that have been damaged by a blow.

browser.definition: A person or animal who browses.

bud.definition: A newly sprouted leaf or blossom that has not yet unfolded.
bud.example: After a long, cold winter, the trees finally began to produce buds.
bud.exampleZh: 經過一個漫長寒冷的冬天，樹木終於開始發芽了。

bulimia.definition: A chronic eating disorder characterized by a binge-and-purge cycle - extreme overeating followed by self-induced vomiting.
bulimia.example: Binging and purging are signs that someone may have bulimia.
bulimia.exampleZh: 暴食和暴食是某人可能患有貪食症的跡象。

budget.definition: Of or relating to a budget.
budget.example: We flew on a budget airline.
budget.exampleZh: 我們搭乘廉價航空公司的班機。

bully.example: You shouldn't bully people for being gay.
bully.exampleZh: 你不應該因為別人是同性戀而欺負他們。

bum.example: Okay, everyone sit on your bum and try and touch your toes.
bum.exampleZh: 好的，每個人坐在你的屁股上，試著觸摸你的腳趾。

bumper.example: The company sells screw-on rubber bumpers and feet.
bumper.exampleZh: 該公司銷售旋入式橡膠保險桿和腳。

burden.definition: To encumber with a literal or figurative burden.
burden.example: to burden a nation with taxes

bun.definition: A small bread roll, often sweetened or spiced.

bureaucracy.definition: Government by bureaus or their administrators or officers.
bureaucracy.example: At that time the administration replaced the system of patronage in the civil service with a bureaucracy.
bureaucracy.exampleZh: 當時，政府以官僚機構取代了公務員體系中的恩賜制度。

burdensome.definition: Characteristic of a burden; arduous or demanding
burdensome.example: When life is so burdensome death has become a sought after refuge.
burdensome.exampleZh: 當生活如此繁重時，死亡就成了人們尋求的避難所。

burglary.definition: The crime of unlawfully breaking into a vehicle, house, store, or other enclosure with the intent to steal.
burglary.example: Ziri was involved in the burglary.
burglary.exampleZh: 齊裡參與了這起入室盜竊案。

burgle.example: Leon was looking to burgle Amelia's house.
burgle.exampleZh: 萊昂想要偷竊阿米莉亞的房子。

bustle.definition: To move busily and energetically with fussiness (often followed by about).
bustle.example: The commuters bustled about inside the train station.
bustle.exampleZh: 火車站內，乘客們熙熙攘攘。

burial.example: Why do Muslims place such importance on burial?
burial.exampleZh: 為什麼穆斯林如此重視喪葬？

bustling.definition: Busy; full of energy and noisy activity.
bustling.example: The commuters bustled about inside the train station.
bustling.exampleZh: 火車站內，乘客們熙熙攘攘。

butcher.definition: To slaughter (animals) and prepare (meat) for market.
butcher.example: The band at that bar really butchered "Hotel California".
butcher.exampleZh: 那個酒吧的樂團真是屠殺了《加州旅館》。

buzz.definition: A continuous, humming noise, as of bees; a confused murmur, as of general conversation in low tones.
buzz.example: Still feeling the buzz from the coffee, he pushed through the last of the homework.
buzz.exampleZh: 他仍然感受到咖啡的嗡嗡聲，繼續完成最後一份作業。

cable.definition: (material) A long object used to make a physical connection.
cable.example: I tried to watch the movie last night but my cable was out.
cable.exampleZh: 昨晚我想看這部電影，但我的電纜沒電了。

cabinet.definition: A storage closet either separate from, or built into, a wall.

campaign.definition: A series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal.
campaign.example: The company is targeting children in their latest advertising campaign.
campaign.exampleZh: 該公司在最新的廣告活動中瞄準了兒童。

cane.definition: A plant with simple stems, like bamboo or sugar cane, or the stem thereof
cane.exampleZh: 穆切斯特流浪者隊以 10-0 慘敗。

candidate.definition: A person who is running in an election.

cannonball.example: The first human cannonball was a 14-year-old girl called "Zazel".
cannonball.exampleZh: 第一個人類砲彈是一位名叫「札澤爾」的14歲女孩。

canon.definition: A generally accepted principle; a rule.
canon.example: The trial must proceed according to the canons of law.
canon.exampleZh: 審判必須按照法律規範進行。

caption.definition: The descriptive heading or title of a document or part therof
caption.example: Only once the drawing is done will the letterer caption it.
caption.exampleZh: 只有當繪圖完成後，書寫者才會為其添加標題。

capability.definition: The power or ability to generate an outcome

caravan.definition: A convoy or procession of travelers, their vehicles and cargo, and any pack animals, especially camels crossing a desert.
caravan.example: The wedding party got in their cars and caravaned from the chapel to the reception hall.
caravan.exampleZh: 婚禮派對上他們的車，從教堂開往接待廳。

captive.definition: One who has been captured or is otherwise confined.

carbon.definition: The chemical element (symbol C) with an atomic number of 6. It can be found in pure form for example as graphite, a black, shiny and very soft material, or diamond, a colourless, transparent, crystalline solid and the hardest known material.
carbon.example: A methane molecule is made up of a single carbon with four hydrogens.
carbon.exampleZh: 甲烷分子由一個碳和四個氫組成。

cardigan.definition: A type of sweater or jumper that fastens up the front with buttons or a zipper, usually machine- or hand-knitted from wool.
cardigan.example: The advantage of a cardigan is that you don't have to pull it over your head.
cardigan.exampleZh: 開襟衫的優點是你不必把它拉到頭上。

carefree.definition: Without cares or worries; free of concern or worries; without difficulty.
carefree.example: He left his cell phone behind and enjoyed a carefree summer day at the beach.
carefree.exampleZh: 他放下手機，在海灘享受了無憂無慮的夏日。

caring.example: She's a very caring person; she always has a kind word for everyone.
caring.exampleZh: 她是一個非常有愛心的人；她總是對每個人說好話。

cardboard.definition: A wood-based material resembling heavy paper, used in the manufacture of boxes, cartons and signs.

carnival.definition: Any of a number of festivals held just before the beginning of Lent.

carpool.definition: An arrangement whereby several people travel together in the same car in order to save costs, reduce pollution etc.

cascade.definition: A waterfall or series of small waterfalls.
cascade.example: Mary swishes off her scarf, and waves and waves of black hair cascade down.
cascade.exampleZh: 瑪莉甩掉圍巾，一縷縷黑髮如瀑布般垂落下來。

carve.example: You carve the roast and I'll serve the vegetables.

cast.example: The area near the stream was covered with little bubbly worm casts.
cast.exampleZh: 靠近溪流的地方佈滿了小泡泡狀的蟲糞。

casually.exampleZh: 雅尼若無其事地開車走了。

catalyst.definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process.
catalyst.example: Economic development and integration are working as a catalyst for peace.
catalyst.exampleZh: 經濟發展和一體化正在成為和平的催化劑。

catastrophe.definition: Any large and disastrous event of great significance
catastrophe.exampleZh: 專家警告可能會發生災難。

catastrophic.definition: Of or pertaining to a catastrophe.

catchy.definition: Instantly appealing and memorable (of a tune or phrase).

catering.definition: The business of providing food and related services; foodservice.
catering.exampleZh: Benedito飯店的餐廳提供餐飲服務。

cause.definition: (often with of, typically of adverse results) The source of, or reason for, an event or action; that which produces or effects a result.
cause.example: They identified a burst pipe as the cause of the flooding.
cause.exampleZh: 他們確定水管爆裂是導致洪水的原因。

cathedral.definition: Relating to the throne or the see of a bishop.
cathedral.exampleZh: 大教堂在城市景觀中佔據主導地位。

cease.example: And with that, his twitching ceased.
cease.exampleZh: 就這樣，他的抽搐停止了。

ceiling.definition: The overhead closure of a room.
ceiling.example: the dining room had an ornate ceiling
ceiling.exampleZh: 餐廳有一個華麗的天花板

cellar.definition: An enclosed underground space, often under a building, used for storage or shelter.

cello.definition: A large stringed instrument of the violin family with four strings, tuned from lowest to highest C-G-D-A, and played with a bow, also possessing an endpin to support the instrument's weight.
cello.exampleZh: 他的大提琴演奏得非常好。

centigrade.definition: A centigrade temperature scale having the freezing point of water defined as 0° and its boiling point defined as 100° at standard atmospheric pressure. Known as the Celsius scale since 1948.

cemetery.definition: A place where the dead are buried; a graveyard or memorial park.
cemetery.exampleZh: 德米特里發現了一個墓地。

certificate.definition: A document containing a certified statement.

certification.exampleZh: 我找到我的出生證明了。

certify.definition: To attest to (a fact) as the truth.
certify.example: These blankets have been certified as fireproof.
certify.exampleZh: 這些毯子已通過防火認證。

chancellor.definition: A senior secretary or official with administrative or legal duties, sometimes in charge of some area of government such as finance or justice.
chancellor.example: Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster  Lord Chancellor
chancellor.exampleZh: 蘭開斯特公國大臣  大法官

challenge.definition: To invite (someone) to take part in a competition.
challenge.example: We challenged the boys next door to a game of football.
challenge.exampleZh: 我們向隔壁的男孩們發起了一場足球比賽。

chaotic.exampleZh: 生活本來就是混亂的嗎？

charming.example: He charmed her with his dashing tales of his days as a sailor.
charming.exampleZh: 他用自己當水手時的精彩故事迷住了她。

characteristic.definition: Being a distinguishing feature of a person or thing.
characteristic.example: The characteristic of a field, if non-zero, must be a prime number.
characteristic.exampleZh: 字段的特徵如果非零，則必須是素數。

chase.example: I need something to chase this shot with.
chase.exampleZh: 我需要一些東西來追逐這張照片。

cheat.definition: To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.
cheat.example: My brother flunked biology because he cheated on his mid-term.
cheat.exampleZh: 我哥哥生物沒及格，因為他期中考作弊。

checkpoint.definition: A point or place where a check is performed, especially a point along a road or on a frontier where travellers are stopped for inspection
checkpoint.example: The travellers were stopped at the checkpoint.

cheeky.definition: Impudent; impertinent; impertinently bold, often in a way that is regarded as endearing or amusing.
cheeky.exampleZh: 唐納德開始表現得厚顏無恥。

cheerfully.example: The word "cheerfully" is commonly used in formal English.
cheerfully.exampleZh: 「愉快地」一詞在正式英語中常用。

cheerfulness.definition: The state of being cheerful; joy.
cheerfulness.example: The word "cheerfulness" is commonly used in formal English.
cheerfulness.exampleZh: 「歡樂」一詞在正式英語中常用。

cherished.definition: Highly valued, having a high personal value.

chemotherapy.definition: Any chemical treatment intended to be therapeutic with respect to a disease state.
chemotherapy.example: Many cancer patients lose their hair because of chemotherapy.
chemotherapy.exampleZh: 許多癌症患者因化療而掉髮。

chew.definition: To crush with the teeth by repeated closing and opening of the jaws; done to food to soften it and break it down by the action of saliva before it is swallowed.
chew.example: Make sure to chew thoroughly, and don't talk with your mouth full!
chew.exampleZh: 一定要充分咀嚼，不要滿嘴說話！

cherry.definition: A small fruit, usually red, black or yellow, with a smooth hard seed and a short hard stem.

chief.definition: A leader or head of a group of people, organisation, etc.
chief.example: All firefighters report to the fire chief.
chief.exampleZh: 所有消防員均向消防隊長報告。

childish.definition: Of or suitable for a child.
childish.example: Your childish temper tantrums are not going to change my decision on this matter.
childish.exampleZh: 你孩子氣的脾氣不會改變我在這件事上的決定。

chimney.definition: A vertical tube or hollow column used to emit environmentally polluting gaseous and solid matter (including but not limited to by-products of burning carbon or hydrocarbon based fuels); a flue.

chip.definition: A small piece broken from a larger piece of solid material.
chip.example: This cup has a chip in it.
chip.exampleZh: 這個杯子裡有一個晶片。

chop.definition: To cut into pieces with short, vigorous cutting motions.

chunk.definition: A part of something that has been separated.

circuit.definition: The act of moving or revolving around, or as in a circle or orbit; a revolution

circumstance.definition: That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.
circumstance.exampleZh: 我們的情況明天就會改變。

circuitry.definition: A specific system of electrical circuits in a particular device; the design of such a system.
circuitry.example: The android maintained an anhydrous core to protect its quantum circuitry.
circuitry.exampleZh: 機器人保留了一個無水核心來保護其量子電路。

citywide.example: They maintained order during the citywide emergency drill.
citywide.exampleZh: 他們在全市應急演練期間維持秩序。

clam.definition: A bivalve mollusk of many kinds, especially those that are edible; for example the soft-shell clam (Mya arenaria), the hard clam (Mercenaria mercenaria), the sea clam or hen clam (Spisula solidissima), and other species. The name is said to have been given originally to the Tridacna gigas, a huge East Indian bivalve.
clam.example: Those sneakers cost me fifty clams!
clam.exampleZh: 那雙運動鞋花了我五十蛤！

clap.definition: To strike the palms of the hands together, creating a sharp sound.
clap.example: The children began to clap in time with the music.
clap.exampleZh: 孩子們開始隨著音樂拍手。

clarify.definition: (of liquids, such as wine or syrup) To make clear or bright by freeing from feculent matter
clarify.example: Leave the wine for 24 hours and it will clarify.
clarify.exampleZh: 將酒放置 24 小時，它就會澄清。

classic.definition: A perfect and/or early example of a particular style.
classic.example: Users who dislike the new visual layout can return to classic mode.
classic.exampleZh: 不喜歡新視覺佈局的使用者可以返回經典模式。

classification.definition: The act of forming into a class or classes; a distribution into groups, as classes, orders, families, etc., according to some common relations or attributes.
classification.example: Not every racist country has the same racial classification system.
classification.exampleZh: 並非每個種族主義國家都有相同的種族分類制度。

clause.definition: (grammar) A verb, its necessary grammatical arguments, and any adjuncts affecting them.

clerical.definition: Of or relating to clerks or their work.
clerical.example: I find it so strange that keyboards can be both musical and clerical.
clerical.exampleZh: 我覺得很奇怪，鍵盤可以是音樂鍵盤，也可以是文字鍵盤。

client.definition: A customer, a buyer or receiver of goods or services.

clog.definition: To block or slow passage through (often with 'up').

closure.definition: An event or occurrence that signifies an ending.
closure.example: the closure of a door, or of a chink

clothing.definition: Any of a wide variety of articles, usually made of fabrics, animal hair, animal skin, or some combination thereof, used to cover the human body for warmth, to preserve modesty, or for fashion.
clothing.example: The clothing and unclothing of the idols was of special significance.
clothing.exampleZh: 偶像的衣服和脫衣具有特殊的意義。

clumsy.definition: Awkward, lacking coordination, not graceful, not dextrous.
clumsy.example: He's very clumsy. I wouldn't trust him with carrying the dishes.
clumsy.exampleZh: 他非常笨拙。我不相信他會端盤子。

coaching.definition: The process by which someone is coached or tutored; instruction.
coaching.example: She has coached many opera stars.
coaching.exampleZh: 她曾指導過許多歌劇明星。

coach.example: She has coached many opera stars.
coach.exampleZh: 她曾指導過許多歌劇明星。

coalition.definition: A temporary group or union of organizations, usually formed for a particular advantage.
coalition.example: The Liberal Democrats and Conservative parties formed a coalition government in 2010.
coalition.exampleZh: 2010年，自由民主黨和保守黨組成聯合政府。

coarse.definition: Composed of large parts or particles; of inferior quality or appearance; not fine in material or close in texture.

coastline.definition: The shape, outline, or boundary of a coast.
coastline.example: Djibouti's coastline is crowded with foreign bases.
coastline.exampleZh: 吉布地的海岸線上佈滿了外國基地。

cocktail.example: Scientists found a cocktail of pollutants in the river downstream from the chemical factory.
cocktail.exampleZh: 科學家在化工廠下游的河流中發現了多種污染物。

cocoa.definition: The dried and partially fermented fatty seeds of the cacao tree from which chocolate is made.

coconut.definition: A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.

coherence.definition: The quality of cohering, or being coherent; internal consistency.

coherent.definition: Unified; sticking together; making up a whole.

coincide.definition: To occupy exactly the same space.
coincide.exampleZh: 這兩個正方形很好重合。

coincidence.definition: Of objects, the property of being coincident; occurring at the same time or place.
coincidence.exampleZh: 多麼令人愉快的巧合啊！

collapse.definition: To break apart and fall down suddenly; to cave in.
collapse.example: Pyramid schemes tend to generate profits for a while and then collapse.
collapse.exampleZh: 傳銷往往會產生一段時間的利潤，然後就崩潰了。

collector.definition: A person who or thing that collects, or which creates or manages a collection.
collector.example: He is an avid collector of nineteenth-century postage stamps.
collector.exampleZh: 他是十九世紀郵票的狂熱收藏家。

colleague.definition: A fellow member of a profession, staff, academic faculty or other organization; an associate.
colleague.example: Young Fortinbras,/ Holding a weak supposal of our worth/...Colleagued with the dream of his advantage,/...hath not failed to pester us with message/ Importing the surrender of those lands/Lost by his father. - Hamlet (Act I, Scene 2)
colleague.exampleZh: 年輕的福丁布拉斯，/對我們的價值抱有微弱的假設/...與他的優勢夢想同行，/...不遺餘力地用信息糾纏我們/進口那些土地的投降/被他父親失去。 - 哈姆雷特（第一幕第二場）

collocation.definition: The grouping or juxtaposition of things, especially words or sounds.
collocation.example: A collocation consists of words that are often found together.
collocation.exampleZh: 搭配由經常一起出現的單字組成。

colonial.definition: Of or pertaining to a colony.
colonial.exampleZh: 英國建立了許多殖民地。

comb.definition: (especially of hair or fur) To groom with a toothed implement; chiefly with a comb.
comb.example: I need to comb my hair before we leave the house
comb.exampleZh: 我們離開家前我需要梳理一下頭髮

coma.definition: A state of unconsciousness from which one may not wake up, usually induced by some form of trauma.

comfortably.example: I snuggled comfortably into the blankets.
comfortably.exampleZh: 我舒舒服服地躺在毯子裏。

commander.definition: One who exercises control and direction of a military or naval organization.

comment.example: I wish I'd commented this complicated algorithm back when I remembered how it worked.
comment.exampleZh: 我希望當我記得這個複雜的演算法是如何運作的時候我就對其進行了評論。

commemorate.definition: To honour the memory of someone or something with a ceremony or object.
commemorate.example: On November 11th we commemorate the fallen with a march.
commemorate.exampleZh: 11 月 11 日，我們舉行遊行紀念陣亡將士。

commercial.definition: An advertisement in a common media format, usually radio or television.

commission.definition: A sending or mission (to do or accomplish something).
commission.example: David received his commission after graduating from West Point.
commission.exampleZh: 大衛從西點軍校畢業後獲得了任命。

commerce.definition: The exchange or buying and selling of commodities; especially the exchange of merchandise, on a large scale, between different places or communities; extended trade or traffic.
commerce.example: Many small business owners belong to a chamber of commerce.
commerce.exampleZh: 許多小企業主屬於商會。

community.definition: A group sharing a common understanding, and often the same language, law, manners, and/or tradition.

commute.definition: To exchange substantially; to abate but not abolish completely, a penalty, obligation, or payment in return for a great, single thing or an aggregate; to cash in; to lessen
commute.example: to commute tithes into rentcharges for a sum; to commute market rents for a premium, to commute daily fares for a season ticket
commute.exampleZh: 將什一稅轉換為一筆租金；以溢價換算市場租金，以季票換算每日票價

commonwealth.example: The British Commonwealth of Nations is unable to let go of power.

compassion.definition: Deep awareness of the suffering of another, coupled with the wish to relieve it.
compassion.exampleZh: 同情心應該凌駕於判決之上。

compassionate.definition: Having, feeling or showing compassion (to or toward someone).

compensate.definition: To do (something good) after (something bad) happens
compensate.example: It is hard work, but they will compensate you well for it.
compensate.exampleZh: 雖然工作很辛苦，但他們會給你很好的補償。

compatible.example: This printer isn't compatible with my computer.
