gravitation.definition: The fundamental force of attraction that exists between all particles with mass in the universe. It is the weakest of the four forces, and possesses a gauge boson known as the graviton.
gravitation.example: He found himself gravitating towards that Muslim girl.
gravitation.exampleZh: 他發現自己被那個穆斯林女孩所吸引。

grief.example: She was worn out from so much grief.
grief.exampleZh: 她因如此悲痛而疲憊不堪。

grieve.definition: To cause sorrow or distress to.

grim.definition: Dismal and gloomy, cold and forbidding
grim.example: Life was grim in many northern industrial towns.
grim.exampleZh: 許多北方工業城鎮的生活十分嚴峻。

grip.definition: To take hold of, particularly with the hand.
grip.example: After a few slips, the tires gripped the pavement.
grip.exampleZh: 經過幾次打滑後，輪胎緊緊抓住了路面。

grin.definition: A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
grin.example: Why do you grin?  Did I say something funny?
grin.exampleZh: 為什麼笑？  我說了什麼好笑的話嗎？

gross.definition: (of behaviour considered to be wrong) Highly or conspicuously offensive.
gross.example: a gross mistake;  gross injustice;  gross negligence; a gross insult
gross.exampleZh: 嚴重錯誤；  嚴重不公正；  重大過失；嚴重侮辱

growing.example: Milk is good for growing children.
growing.exampleZh: 牛奶對成長中的孩子有好處。

guarantee.definition: To give an assurance that something will be done right.
guarantee.example: The long sunny days guarantee a good crop.
guarantee.exampleZh: 漫長的晴天保證了豐收。

guard.definition: To protect from danger; to secure against surprise, attack, or injury; to keep in safety; to defend.

guerrilla.definition: A soldier in a small independent group, fighting against the government or regular forces by surprise raids.
guerrilla.example: It's not "gorilla rain", but "guerrilla rain"; do you understand?
guerrilla.exampleZh: 這不是“大猩猩雨”，而是“遊擊雨”；你懂嗎？

guideline.definition: A non-specific rule or principle that provides direction to action or behaviour.
guideline.example: He considered the Ten Commandments more a guideline than a requirement.
guideline.exampleZh: 他認為十誡更多的是一個指導方針，而不是一個要求。

gum.definition: (often in the plural) The flesh around the teeth.

gulp.exampleZh: 你沒把三明治吞下去嗎？

guts.definition: The alimentary canal, especially the intestine.

gunshot.definition: The act of discharging a firearm.

habitant.definition: A member of habitation colony at Stadacona founded by Samuel de Champlain, where Quebec City now lies

hairy.definition: Of a person, having a lot of hair on the body.

hail.definition: Of hail, to fall from the sky.
hail.example: They say it's going to hail tomorrow.

hammer.definition: To strike repeatedly with a hammer, some other implement, the fist, etc.
hammer.example: Tony hammered on the door to try to get him to open.
hammer.exampleZh: 東尼用力敲門，試圖讓他開門。

handicap.definition: Something that prevents, hampers, or hinders.

handicraft.definition: A trade requiring skill of hand; manual occupation; handcraft.
handicraft.example: I took a handicraft course last year.
handicraft.exampleZh: 我去年參加了手工藝課程。

handout.definition: A worksheet, leaflet, or pamphlet that is given out (usually by hand) for a certain use.
handout.example: It's a place to get a hand up, not a handout.
handout.exampleZh: 這是一個舉手的地方，而不是施捨的地方。

handrail.definition: A rail which can be held, such as on the side of a staircase, ramp or other walkway, and serving as a support or guard.
handrail.example: The staircase to the loft doesn't have a handrail. It's not safe.
handrail.exampleZh: 通往閣樓的樓梯沒有扶手。這不安全。

hang.definition: The way in which something hangs.
hang.example: This skirt has a nice hang.
hang.exampleZh: 這條裙子的懸垂感很好。

harm.example: No harm came to my possessions.
harm.exampleZh: 我的財產沒有受到任何損害。

hardware.definition: Fixtures, equipment, tools and devices used for general-purpose construction and repair of a structure or object. Also such equipment as sold as stock by a store of the same name, e.g. hardware store.

harmless.definition: Incapable of causing harm or danger; safe.

harrow.definition: A device consisting of a heavy framework having several disks or teeth in a row, which is dragged across ploughed land to smooth or break up the soil, to remove weeds or cover seeds; a harrow plow.
harrow.example: The horses are pulling the harrow.

haste.example: We were running late so we finished our meal in haste.
haste.exampleZh: 我們遲到了，所以我們匆匆吃完飯。

hatching.definition: A method of shading areas of a drawing or diagram with fine parallel lines.
hatching.example: We got a good hatching from the swallowtail eggs.
hatching.exampleZh: 我們的燕尾蛋孵化得很好。

hastily.definition: In a hasty manner; quickly, hurriedly.

haunt.definition: To inhabit, or visit frequently (most often used in reference to ghosts).
haunt.example: A couple of ghosts haunt the old, burnt-down house.
haunt.exampleZh: 幾個鬼魂在這棟被燒毀的老房子裡出沒。

haunting.definition: Remaining in the mind; not easily forgotten.
haunting.exampleZh: 他有一種令人難以忘懷的目光。

hay.definition: Grass cut and dried for use as animal fodder.

hazardous.definition: Risky, dangerous, with the nature of a hazard.

heap.definition: A crowd; a throng; a multitude or great number of people.
heap.example: a heap of earth or stones

headquarters.definition: The military installation from which troops are commanded and orders are issued; the military unit consisting of a commander and his support staff.
headquarters.exampleZh: 我們的總部將於明天開幕。

hedge.definition: A thicket of bushes or other shrubbery, especially one planted as a fence between two portions of land, or to separate the parts of a garden.
hedge.example: He trims the hedge once a week.

heartbreaking.definition: That causes great grief, anguish or distress.
heartbreaking.exampleZh: 丹揭露了令人心碎的真相。

helicopter.definition: An aircraft that is borne along by one or more sets of long rotating blades which allow it to hover, move in any direction including reverse, or land; and typically having a smaller set of blades on its tail that stabilize the aircraft.
helicopter.example: We flew over the city in a helicopter.
helicopter.exampleZh: 我們乘坐直升機飛越城市上空。

concur.definition: To unite or agree (in action or opinion); to have a common opinion; to coincide; to correspond.
concur.example: I concur with all you are saying.

avid.definition: Enthusiastic; keen; eager; showing great interest in something or desire to do something

wing.definition: An appendage of an animal's (bird, bat, insect) body that enables it to fly; a similar fin at the side of a ray or similar fish

marvel.definition: To become filled with wonderment or admiration; to be amazed at something.

organizational.definition: Of, relating to, or produced by an organization.

enviable.definition: Arousing or likely to arouse envy.
enviable.example: If someone is not envied, they don't have enviable qualities.
enviable.exampleZh: 如果一個人不被羨慕，那麼他就沒有令人羨慕的特質。

psychiatrist.definition: A medical doctor specializing in psychiatry.

clinical.definition: Of or pertaining to a medical clinic or facility.
clinical.example: Medicine is now more often practiced in a clinical setting than in the home.
clinical.exampleZh: 現在，醫學更多的是在臨床環境中而不是在家裡進行。

corps.definition: A battlefield formation composed of two or more divisions.

forensic.definition: Relating to the use of science and technology in the investigation and establishment of facts or evidence in a court of law.
forensic.example: A forensic medical examination has been designated.

juvenile.example: Paul was charged with juvenile breaking and entering.
juvenile.exampleZh: 保羅被指控犯有青少年闖入罪。

jurisdiction.definition: The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply the law.
jurisdiction.example: The judge said that the case was outside of his jurisdiction.
jurisdiction.exampleZh: 法官表示，此案不屬於他的管轄範圍。

accustomed.definition: (of a person) Familiar with something through repeated experience; adapted to existing conditions.

traumatic.definition: Of, caused by, or causing trauma.

trauma.definition: Any serious injury to the body, often resulting from violence or an accident.
trauma.exampleZh: 嚴重的創傷使事情變得困難。

constraint.example: I think I've showed considerable constraint under the circumstances.
constraint.exampleZh: 我認為在這種情況下我表現出了相當大的克制。

eccentric.definition: Not at or in the centre; away from the centre.
eccentric.example: As of 2008, Margaret had the most eccentric orbit of any moon in the solar system, though Nereid's mean eccentricity is greater.
eccentric.exampleZh: 截至 2008 年，瑪格麗特號的軌道是太陽系中所有衛星中最偏心率的，儘管海衛二的平均偏心率更大。

conform.definition: (of persons, often followed by to) To act in accordance with expectations; to behave in the manner of others, especially as a result of social pressure.
conform.example: Most humans actually hallucinate to conform.
conform.exampleZh: 大多數人實際上產生了順從的幻覺。

norms.definition: That which is normal or typical.
norms.example: Unemployment is the norm in this part of the country.
norms.exampleZh: 失業是該國這一地區的常態。

sanctity.definition: Holiness of life or disposition; saintliness
sanctity.example: Pornography undermines the sanctity of marriage and family values.
sanctity.exampleZh: 色情內容破壞了婚姻和家庭價值的神聖性。

quirk.definition: An idiosyncrasy; a slight glitch, mannerism; something unusual about the manner or style of something or someone
quirk.example: The car steers cleanly, but the gearshift has a few quirks.
quirk.exampleZh: 汽車轉向乾淨俐落，但換檔裝置有一些怪癖。

oddity.definition: An odd or strange thing or opinion.
oddity.exampleZh: 博物館裡也有一些奇怪的東西。

compel.example: Logic compels the wise, while fools feel compelled by emotions.
compel.exampleZh: 邏輯強迫智者，而傻瓜則被情感強迫。

compulsion.definition: An irrational need or irresistible urge to perform some action, often despite negative consequences.
compulsion.example: During the basketball game, I had a sudden compulsion to have a smoke.
compulsion.exampleZh: 打籃球的時候，我突然想抽煙。

assertion.definition: The act of asserting; positive declaration or averment.
assertion.example: You're a man of strong assertions!
assertion.exampleZh: 你是一個有強烈主張的人！

psyche.definition: The human soul, mind, or spirit.

individuality.definition: The characteristics that contribute to the differentiation or distinction of someone or something from a group of otherwise comparable identity.
individuality.exampleZh: 每個人對情況的解釋不同。

conformity.definition: The state of things being similar or identical.
conformity.exampleZh: 我選擇真實性而不是順從性。

rally.definition: To collect, and reduce to order, as troops dispersed or thrown into confusion; to gather again; to reunite.

congregate.definition: To collect into an assembly or assemblage; to bring into one place, or into a united body
congregate.example: Eagles congregate here in the spring.

resent.definition: To feel resentment over; to consider as an affront.
resent.example: The bride greatly resented being left at the church.
resent.exampleZh: 新娘對被留在教堂感到非常不滿。

wary.definition: Cautious of danger; carefully watching and guarding against deception, trickery, and dangers; suspiciously prudent

self-conscious.definition: Aware of oneself as an individual being.

stoke.example: We want our cold nights in Stoke.
stoke.exampleZh: 我們想要在斯托克度過寒冷的夜晚。

hostess.example: The host and hostess greeted their guests at the door.
hostess.exampleZh: 男女主人在門口迎接客人。

dynamically.definition: Of a dynamic nature; variable or constantly changing nature.
dynamically.example: The word "dynamically" is commonly used in formal English.
dynamically.exampleZh: 「動態」一詞在正式英語中常用。

perpetuate.definition: To make perpetual; to preserve from extinction or oblivion.
perpetuate.example: Pornography perpetuates harmful stereotypes about women.
perpetuate.exampleZh: 色情作品延續了對女性有害的刻板印象。

naturalness.definition: The state or quality of being natural.
naturalness.example: The word "naturalness" is commonly used in formal English.
naturalness.exampleZh: 「自然」一詞在正式英語中常用。

exhibitionist.definition: A student at secondary school or university who has been awarded an exhibition. The exhibition usually involves a financial prize and may include the right to wear a distinctive gown, especially at the University of Oxford.
exhibitionist.example: An exhibitionist would be the ideal partner for a voyeur.
exhibitionist.exampleZh: 暴露狂將是偷窺狂的理想伴侶。

isolate.definition: To set apart or cut off from others.

isolated.definition: Placed or standing apart or alone; in isolation.

humble.definition: To defeat or reduce the power, independence, or pride of
humble.example: He lives in a humble one-bedroom cottage.
humble.exampleZh: 他住在一間簡陋的一房小屋裡。

vegetation.example: There were large amounts of vegetation in the forest.

brief.definition: To summarize a recent development to some person with decision-making power.
brief.example: The U.S. president was briefed on the military coup and its implications on African stability.
brief.exampleZh: 美國總統聽取了有關軍事政變及其對非洲穩定影響的簡報。

suffice.definition: To be enough or sufficient; to meet the need (of anything); to be adequate; to be good enough.
suffice.example: For this plum cake, two eggs should suffice.
suffice.exampleZh: 對於這個梅子蛋糕，兩個雞蛋就足夠了。

respite.definition: A brief interval of rest or relief.
respite.example: Go forth! Without fear and without respite!
respite.exampleZh: 前進吧！沒有恐懼，沒有喘息！

gruelling.definition: So difficult or taxing as to make one exhausted; backbreaking.
gruelling.example: Labourers on plantations often face gruelling hours, low pay, and no job security.
gruelling.exampleZh: 種植園裡的勞工常常面臨艱苦的工作時間、低薪和沒有工作保障的情況。

comprise.definition: To be made up of; to consist of (especially a comprehensive list of parts).
comprise.example: The parts are comprised by the whole.

plaster.definition: A paste applied to the skin for healing or cosmetic purposes.

darkroom.definition: A dark room, where photographs are developed.
darkroom.example: What are you looking for in the darkroom?

induction.example: Induction cookers use magnets to directly heat pots and pans.
induction.exampleZh: 電磁爐利用磁鐵直接加熱鍋碗瓢盆。

blister.definition: A small bubble between the layers of the skin that contains watery or bloody fluid and is caused by friction and pressure, burning, freezing, chemical irritation, disease or infection.

sponsorship.definition: The state or practice of being a sponsor.
sponsorship.example: I find that for me, my sponsorship of a child via a charitable NGO provides a sense of great satisfaction.
sponsorship.exampleZh: 我發現對我來說，透過慈善非政府組織贊助一個孩子給我帶來了極大的滿足感。

ego.definition: The self, especially with a sense of self-importance.
ego.exampleZh: 社會有一個脆弱的自我。

class.definition: To assign to a class; to classify.
class.example: I would class this with most of the other mediocre works of the period.
class.exampleZh: 我會將其與該時期大多數其他平庸的作品歸為一類。

saloon.definition: A large room, especially one used to receive and entertain guests.
saloon.example: A pint of beer in the saloon bar costs a penny more than in the public bar.
saloon.exampleZh: 酒吧裡的一品脫啤酒比公共酒吧貴一分錢。

lap.definition: The loose part of a coat; the lower part of a garment that plays loosely; a skirt; an apron.
lap.example: The boy was sitting on his mother's lap.

beforehand.definition: At an earlier or preceding time.
beforehand.example: Will it be possible to have access to the room beforehand so that we can set up chairs?
beforehand.exampleZh: 可以提前進入房間以便我們安排椅子嗎？

slob.example: Daniel is a fat, lazy slob.
slob.exampleZh: 丹尼爾是個又胖又懶的懶漢。

coverage.definition: An amount by which something or someone is covered.
coverage.example: Before laying sod on that clay, the ground needs two inches of coverage with topsoil.
coverage.exampleZh: 在黏土上鋪草皮之前，地面需要覆蓋兩英吋的表土。

gadget.definition: A thing whose name cannot be remembered; thingamajig, doohickey.
gadget.example: He bought a neat new gadget for shredding potatoes.
gadget.exampleZh: 他買了一個漂亮的新玩意兒，用來切馬鈴薯絲。

unplug.definition: To disconnect from a supply, especially an electrical socket.
unplug.example: To save electricity, I unplug my computer when I am not at home.
unplug.exampleZh: 為了省電費，我不在家時會拔掉電腦電源。

rediscover.definition: To discover again; especially something previously lost or forgotten.
rediscover.example: She rediscovered her faith in religion.
rediscover.exampleZh: 她重新發現了對宗教的信仰。

subtly.definition: With subtleness, in a subtle manner; with cleverness rather than brute force.
subtly.example: If you're going to point out Tom's mistakes, at least do it subtly.
subtly.exampleZh: 如果你要指出湯姆的錯誤，至少要巧妙地指出。

brainwash.definition: To affect one's mind by using extreme mental pressure or any other mind-affecting process. (e.g. hypnosis)
brainwash.exampleZh: 他們對西莉亞進行了洗腦。

implicate.definition: (with “in”) To show to be connected or involved in an unfavorable or criminal way.
implicate.example: The evidence implicates involvement of top management in the scheme.
implicate.exampleZh: 證據顯示高層管理人員參與了該計劃。

craft.definition: To make by hand and with much skill.
craft.example: state crafting; the process of crafting global policing
craft.exampleZh: 國家工藝；制定全球警務的過程

brainwashing.definition: A form of indoctrination that forces people to abandon their beliefs in favour of another set of beliefs by conditioning through various forms of pressure or torture

malfunction.example: She told him the machine was poorly built, but they both knew that she was the one who had malfunctioned.
malfunction.exampleZh: 她告訴他這台機器製造得很差，但他們都知道她出了故障。

wayside.definition: The side of a road or path.
wayside.example: Many wild flowers grew on the wayside.

dependent.definition: One who relies on another for support
dependent.example: With two children and an ailing mother, she had three dependents in all.
dependent.exampleZh: 她有兩個孩子和一個生病的母親，總共有三個家屬。

con.definition: To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.

puzzle.example: We puzzled over the curious-shaped lock, but were unable to discover how the key should be inserted.
puzzle.exampleZh: 我們對形狀奇特的鎖感到困惑，但不知道鑰匙應該如何插入。

carrier.definition: A person or object that carries someone or something else.

ghostly.definition: Of or pertaining to ghosts or spirits.
ghostly.example: The ghostly moaning was heard from upstairs.
ghostly.exampleZh: 樓上傳來幽靈般的呻吟聲。

apt.definition: Suitable; appropriate; fit or fitted; suited.

washtub.definition: A tub used for washing clothes.
washtub.example: He put the clothes in the washtub.
washtub.exampleZh: 他把衣服放進洗衣盆裡。

portable.definition: Able to be carried or easily moved.

ornamental.definition: Serving as an ornament; having no purpose other than to make more beautiful.
ornamental.example: Some think it most ornamental to wear their bracelets on their wrists; others, about their ankles.
ornamental.exampleZh: 有些人認為手鐲戴在手腕上最具觀賞性；其他人，關於他們的腳踝。

unmanageable.definition: Not manageable; not readily submitting to handling or management; not easily restrained, governed, or directed
unmanageable.example: As beasts of labour buffaloes are excessively strong, but slow and unmanageable.
unmanageable.exampleZh: 作為勞動動物，水牛非常強壯，但速度緩慢且難以管理。

appliance.definition: An implement, an instrument or apparatus designed (or at least used) as a means to a specific end (often specified), especially:
appliance.example: Yanni went to the appliance section.

relinquish.definition: To give up, abandon or retire from something. To trade away.

tedious.exampleZh: 乏味的重複會讓老師不高興。

tedium.example: Your language sounds tedious. Your brains are full of tedium.
tedium.exampleZh: 你的語言聽起來很無聊。你的大腦充滿了單調乏味。

exert.example: I exerted myself in today's training.

alleviate.definition: To make less severe, as a pain or difficulty.
alleviate.example: Alcohol is often a cheap tool to alleviate the stress of a hard day.
alleviate.exampleZh: 酒精通常是緩解辛苦一天壓力的廉價工具。

elaborate.example: After reading a long, elaborate description, I was impressed but no wiser.
elaborate.exampleZh: 讀完長篇大論的描述後，我印象深刻，但並沒有更明智。

extortion.definition: The practice of extorting money or other property by the use of force or threats.
extortion.example: Tom was the victim of an extortion attempt.
extortion.exampleZh: 湯姆是勒索企圖的受害者。

elaboration.definition: The act or process of producing or refining with labor; improvement by successive operations; refinement.
elaboration.example: the elaboration of chyle, or sap, or tissues
elaboration.exampleZh: 乳糜、汁液或組織的精製

detestable.definition: Stimulating disgust or detestation; offensive; shocking.
detestable.example: The word "detestable" is commonly used in formal English.

suds.definition: Lather; foam or froth formed by mixing soap and water.
suds.example: We went out for some pizza and suds.
suds.exampleZh: 我們出去吃了一些披薩和肥皂水。

prowl.definition: To rove over, through, or about in a stealthy manner; especially, to search in, as for prey or booty.
prowl.example: It's tough to sneak vandalism into Wikipedia as there are plenty of other users prowling the Recent Changes page.
prowl.exampleZh: 想要在維基百科中偷偷進行破壞是很困難的，因為有很多其他用戶在「最近的更改」頁面上徘徊。

agreeable.definition: Pleasing, either to the mind or senses; pleasant; grateful.

speculate.definition: To think, meditate or reflect on a subject; to consider, to deliberate or cogitate.

speculation.definition: The process of thinking or meditating on a subject.

speculative.definition: Characterized by speculation; based on guessing, unfounded opinions, or extrapolation.
speculative.exampleZh: 這些都是毫無根據的猜測。

disdain.definition: A feeling of contempt or scorn.
disdain.example: The cat viewed the cheap supermarket catfood with disdain and stalked away.
disdain.exampleZh: 那隻貓不屑地看著超市裡的廉價貓糧，揚長而去。

peacock.definition: A male peafowl, especially Pavo cristatus, notable for its brilliant iridescently ocellated tail.
peacock.exampleZh: 孔雀展現出充滿活力的羽毛。

torment.definition: To cause severe suffering to (stronger than to vex but weaker than to torture.)
torment.example: The child tormented the flies by pulling their wings off.
torment.exampleZh: 孩子把蒼蠅的翅膀扯下來折磨它們。

reenact.example: The echo of the American Civil War still resonates in the United States, and especially in a small Pennsylvania town where one of the war’s most significant battles took place. Each year people descend on Gettysburg to remember and reenact that turbulent in time in American history.
reenact.exampleZh: 美國內戰的迴響仍然在美國產生共鳴，特別是在賓夕法尼亞州的一個小鎮，那裡發生了戰爭中最重要的戰鬥之一。每年，人們都會來到葛底斯堡，紀念並重演美國歷史上那段動盪的時期。

exploitation.definition: The act of utilizing something; industry.
exploitation.example: the exploitation of children in beauty pageants
exploitation.exampleZh: 選美比賽中對兒童的剝削

extravagance.definition: Excessive or superfluous expenditure of money.

mechanism.definition: (within a machine or machinery) Any mechanical means for the conversion or control of motion, or the transmission or control of power.

hence.definition: From here, from this place, away

offspring.definition: A person's daughter(s) and/or son(s); a person's children.
offspring.example: Artists often treasure their works as their immortal offspring.
offspring.exampleZh: 藝術家常常將他們的作品視為不朽的後代。

drive.definition: To provide an impetus for motion or other physical change, to move an object by means of the provision of force thereto.
drive.example: You drive nails into wood with a hammer.
drive.exampleZh: 您用錘子將釘子釘入木頭。

oblige.definition: To constrain someone by force or by social, moral or legal means.
oblige.example: I am obliged to report to the police station every week.
oblige.exampleZh: 我有義務每週到警察局報到。

collaborator.definition: A person who works with others towards a common goal.

collaborate.definition: To work together with others to achieve a common goal.
collaborate.example: Let's collaborate on this project, and get it finished faster.
collaborate.exampleZh: 讓我們合作完成這個項目，並更快地完成它。

collaboration.example: Collaboration can be a useful part of the creative process.
collaboration.exampleZh: 協作可以成為創作過程中有用的一部分。

showy.definition: (sometimes derogatory) calling attention; flashy; standing out to the eye
showy.example: I have no cell phone, no showy photos, no funny personality. Just medicaments.
showy.exampleZh: 我沒有手機，沒有艷麗的照片，沒有搞笑的個性。只是藥物。

collaborative.definition: Of, relating to, or done by collaboration.
collaborative.example: No need to thank me - it was a collaborative effort.
collaborative.exampleZh: 無需感謝我——這是大家共同努力的成果。

premise.definition: A proposition antecedently supposed or proved; something previously stated or assumed as the basis of further argument; a condition; a supposition.

wrangle.definition: To bicker, or quarrel angrily and noisily.

parasite.definition: A person who lives on other people's efforts or expense and gives little or nothing back.
parasite.example: Lice, fleas, ticks and mites are widely spread parasites.
parasite.exampleZh: 蝨子、跳蚤、蜱蟲和蟎蟲是廣泛傳播的寄生蟲。

poise.definition: A state of balance, equilibrium or stability.
poise.example: I poised the crowbar in my hand, and waited.
poise.exampleZh: 我把撬棍握在手裡，等待著。

customary.definition: In accordance with, or established by, custom or common usage

notable.definition: Worthy of note; remarkable; memorable; noted or distinguished.
notable.example: In Algiers, the Martyrs' Memorial is a notable landmark.
notable.exampleZh: 在阿爾及爾，烈士紀念碑是一個著名的地標。

vicious.exampleZh: 一場殘酷的監護權之爭隨之而來。

viciously.definition: In a vicious manner; ferociously or maliciously.
viciously.exampleZh: 安東尼奧惡毒地殺了瑞瑪。

pupil.definition: A learner under the supervision of a teacher or professor.

withdrawal.definition: Receiving from someone's care what one has earlier entrusted to them. Usually refers to money.

depict.definition: To render a representation of something, using words, sounds, images, or other means.
depict.example: Reality isn't as you depict it.
depict.exampleZh: 現實並不像你描述的那樣。

upheaval.definition: Change, from one state to another
upheaval.example: The Industrial Revolution caused a great upheaval in society.
upheaval.exampleZh: 工業革命引起了社會的巨大變革。

depiction.definition: A lifelike image of something, either verbal or visual
depiction.example: Japan really is the way Miyazaki depicts it.
depiction.exampleZh: 日本確實就是宮崎駿描繪的那樣。

shortlist.definition: To place something on a short list
shortlist.example: No British authors are on the shortlist for the Booker Prize this year.
shortlist.exampleZh: 今年布克獎的入圍名單中沒有英國作家。

uneventful.definition: Monotonous; lacking significant or noteworthy events
uneventful.example: The majority of our hiking trip was great and uneventful.
uneventful.exampleZh: 我們的徒步旅行的大部分時間都很棒而且平安無事。

pertinent.definition: Important with regard to (a subject or matter); relevant
pertinent.example: Could you please summarize the pertinent points?
pertinent.exampleZh: 能概括一下相關要點嗎？

daydream.definition: A spontaneous and fanciful series of thoughts while awake not connected to immediate reality.
daydream.example: Stop daydreaming and get back to work!
daydream.exampleZh: 別再做白日夢了，回去工作吧！

simplicity.definition: The state or quality of being simple

protagonist.definition: (authorship) The main character, or one of the main characters, in any story, such as a literary work or drama.
protagonist.example: The name of the protagonist is still unknown.

blockbuster.definition: A high-explosive bomb used for the purposes of demolishing extensive areas, such as a city block.
blockbuster.example: Sony Pictures has shared a new video clip from its upcoming blockbuster.
blockbuster.exampleZh: 索尼影業分享了即將上映的大片的新影片剪輯。

sequel.definition: The events, collectively, which follow a previously mentioned event; the aftermath.
sequel.example: Each sequel of this game contains fewer and fewer characters.
sequel.exampleZh: 該遊戲的每個續集包含的角色越來越少。

resonate.definition: To vibrate or sound, especially in response to another vibration.
resonate.example: The books on top of the piano resonate when he plays certain notes.
resonate.exampleZh: 當他彈奏某些音符時，鋼琴頂部的書會產生共鳴。

irony.definition: A statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is written literally; the use of words expressing something other than their literal intention, often in a humorous context.
irony.example: How do you translate the word "irony?"

acidic.definition: Having a pH less than 7, or being sour, or having the strength to neutralize alkalis, or turning a litmus paper red.

align.definition: To form a line; to fall into line.
align.example: When do Earth and Mars align?
align.exampleZh: 地球和火星什麼時候對齊？

manipulation.definition: The practice of manipulating or the state of being manipulated.
manipulation.example: The dealer's manipulations could have removed cards from the deck.
manipulation.exampleZh: 莊家的操縱可能會將牌從牌堆中移除。

alignment.definition: An arrangement of items in a line.

suitor.definition: One who pursues someone, especially a woman, for marriage; a wooer; one who courts someone.
suitor.exampleZh: 薩米是蕾拉的秘密追求者。

deceit.definition: An act or practice intended to deceive; a trick.
deceit.example: The whole conversation was merely a deceit.
deceit.exampleZh: 整個談話只是一個騙局。

persuasively.definition: In a manner intended to convince or persuade.
persuasively.example: The eloquent speaker persuasively stated her argument.
persuasively.exampleZh: 這位雄辯的演講者很有說服力地闡述了她的論點。

alienate.definition: To convey or transfer to another, as title, property, or right; to part voluntarily with ownership of.

minutiae.definition: A minor detail, often of negligible importance.
minutiae.example: They spent all their time on minutiae, never making real progress.
minutiae.exampleZh: 他們把所有的時間都花在細節上，卻從未有過真正的進展。

confinement.definition: The act of confining or the state of being confined.
confinement.example: Years of confinement affected his health.
confinement.exampleZh: 多年的監禁影響了他的健康。

peasant.definition: A member of the lowly social class that toils on the land, constituted by small farmers and tenants, sharecroppers, farmhands and other laborers on the land where they form the main labor force in agriculture and horticulture.

inexplicable.definition: Impossible to explain; not easily accounted for.
inexplicable.example: There was something inexplicable about the story.
inexplicable.exampleZh: 這個故事有一些令人費解的地方。

pigeon.definition: One of several birds of the family Columbidae, which consists of more than 300 species.

inexplicably.definition: In an inexplicable manner; for an unknown reason.
inexplicably.example: William seemed inexplicably angry with Sandra.
inexplicably.exampleZh: 威廉似乎對桑德拉莫名其妙地生氣。

harassment.definition: Persistent attacks and criticism causing worry and distress.
harassment.example: The word "harassment" is commonly used in formal English.
harassment.exampleZh: 「騷擾」一詞在正式英語中常用。

harass.definition: To fatigue or to tire with repeated and exhausting efforts.
harass.example: To harass good people is no different than speaking ill of them.
harass.exampleZh: 騷擾好人就跟說他們壞話沒有什麼不同。

cleverness.example: The word "cleverness" is commonly used in formal English.
cleverness.exampleZh: 「聰明」一詞在正式英語中常用。

readable.definition: (of handwriting, print, etc) legible, possible to read or at least decipher
readable.example: If that sign was still readable we'd know where we are!
readable.exampleZh: 如果那個標誌仍然可讀，我們就會知道我們在哪裡！

persecute.definition: To pursue in a manner to injure, grieve, or afflict; to beset with cruelty or malignity; to harass; especially, to afflict, harass, punish, or put to death for one's race, sexual identity, adherence to a particular religious creed, or mode of worship.
persecute.example: He who persecutes one will persecute all.
persecute.exampleZh: 迫害一個人就會迫害所有人。

smug.definition: Irritatingly pleased with oneself, offensively self-complacent, self-satisfied.
smug.example: Kate looked extremely smug this morning.
smug.exampleZh: 凱特今天早上看起來非常得意。

smugly.example: He was smirking smugly at us.
smugly.exampleZh: 他正對我們得意地笑著。

ferociously.definition: In a ferocious manner, particularly violent and aggressive.

hostile.definition: Not friendly, appropriate to an enemy; showing the disposition of an enemy; showing ill will and malevolence, or a desire to thwart and injure

ferocious.definition: Marked by extreme and violent energy.
ferocious.example: Layla's house was devoured by a ferocious fire.
ferocious.exampleZh: 萊拉的房子被猛烈的大火吞噬了。

hostility.example: My resentment and anger towards you caused hostility and a division between us.
hostility.exampleZh: 我對你的怨恨和憤怒導致了我們之間的敵意和分裂。

dismay.definition: To cause to feel apprehension; great sadness, or fear; to deprive of energy
dismay.example: He looked in dismay at the destruction of the town caused by the hurricane.
dismay.exampleZh: 他沮喪地看著颶風對城鎮造成的破壞。

ruthless.definition: Without pity or compassion; cruel, pitiless.

methodical.definition: In an organized manner; proceeding with regard to method; systematic.
methodical.example: the methodical arrangement of arguments; a methodical treatise
methodical.exampleZh: 有條不紊地安排論點；有條理的論文

ruthlessness.example: The word "ruthlessness" is commonly used in formal English.

clan.definition: A group of people all descended from a common ancestor, in fact or belief.
clan.example: Sami married into the Bakir clan.
clan.exampleZh: 薩米嫁給了巴基爾氏族。

crumble.example: The empire crumbled when the ruler's indiscretions came to light.
crumble.exampleZh: 當統治者的輕率行為曝光後，帝國崩潰了。

crumb.definition: A small piece which breaks off from baked food (such as cake, biscuit or bread).
crumb.example: The pigeons were happily pecking at crumbs of bread on the ground.
crumb.exampleZh: 鴿子們高興地啄食著地上的麵包屑。

pace.definition: To walk back and forth in a small distance.

closeness.definition: The state of being close (all senses)
closeness.example: The word "closeness" is commonly used in formal English.

rarity.definition: A measure of the scarcity of an object.
rarity.example: Because of their rarity, pandas have become a world treasure.
rarity.exampleZh: 由於其稀有性，大熊貓已成為世界瑰寶。

evoke.definition: To call out; to draw out or bring forth.
evoke.example: Being here evokes long forgotten memories.
evoke.exampleZh: 來到這裡喚起了人們早已遺忘的記憶。

repel.definition: To turn (someone) away from a privilege, right, job, etc.
repel.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞擊退了多次外國入侵。

repellant.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞擊退了多次外國入侵。

aqua.example: Aqua Tofana was a poison used in Naples and Rome in the 17th century.
aqua.exampleZh: Aqua Tofana 是 17 世紀那不勒斯和羅馬使用的毒藥。

aquarium.definition: A tank, often made of glass, for keeping live fish or other aquatic animals.
aquarium.exampleZh: 米羅斯拉夫有一個巨大的水族館。

self-worth.definition: The value one assigns to oneself or one's abilities in self assessment.
self-worth.example: Recognizing one's self-worth is a necessary step to respecting the worth of others.
self-worth.exampleZh: 認識自我價值是尊重他人價值的必要步驟。

flashback.definition: (authorship) A dramatic device in which an earlier event is inserted into the normal chronological flow of a narrative.

agonize.definition: To writhe with agony; to suffer violent anguish.
agonize.example: Traveling with Mennad is agonizingly boring.
agonize.exampleZh: 和門納德一起旅行非常無聊。

citation.definition: An official summons or notice given to a person to appear.
citation.example: It's a simple citation of facts.
citation.exampleZh: 這是對事實的簡單引用。

cite.definition: To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.
cite.exampleZh: 始終引用您的消息來源。

recite.definition: To repeat aloud (some passage, poem or other text previously memorized, or in front of one's eyes), often before an audience.

pleasurable.example: The massage was a pleasurable experience.
pleasurable.exampleZh: 按摩是一次愉快的經驗。

ecstasy.example: Stop using emoticons everywhere. It makes it look like you're on ecstasy...
ecstasy.exampleZh: 別再到處使用表情符號了。這讓你看起來像是欣喜若狂…

substantially.definition: In a strong substantial manner; considerably.
substantially.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要進行實質的貨幣改革。

nature.definition: The natural world; that which consists of all things unaffected by or predating human technology, production, and design. (Compare ecosystem.)
nature.exampleZh: 大自然從不說謊（即說謊）。

diversity.definition: The quality of being diverse or different; difference or unlikeness.

grumble.definition: To make a low, growling or rumbling noise, like a hungry stomach or certain animals.

elitist.definition: (usually derogatory) Someone who believes in or is perceived as believing in rule by an elite group.
elitist.example: The words "elitist" and "elitism" are often used by anti-intellectuals.
elitist.exampleZh: 「精英主義」和「精英主義」這兩個詞經常被反知識分子使用。

rumble.definition: To make a low, heavy, continuous sound.
rumble.example: I could hear the thunder rumbling in the distance.

neglectful.definition: Tending to neglect; failing to take care of matters which require attention.

conscientious.definition: Thorough, careful, or vigilant in one’s task performance.
conscientious.example: He was a thoughtful and conscientious worker.
conscientious.exampleZh: 他是一位有思想、盡責的工人。

roundup.definition: An activity in which cattle are herded together in order to be inspected, counted, branded or shipped.
roundup.example: The evening news began with a roundup of the day's top headlines.
roundup.exampleZh: 晚間新聞以當天頭條新聞的綜述開始。

indefinite.definition: Without limit; forever, or until further notice; not definite.

indefinitely.definition: In a manner that is not definite.

fuse.definition: A cord that, when lit, conveys the fire to some explosive device.
fuse.example: When talking about being laid off, he has a short fuse.
fuse.exampleZh: 當談到被解僱時，他脾氣暴躁。

atmospheric.definition: Of, relating to, produced by, or coming from the atmosphere.
atmospheric.exampleZh: 一部黑暗氣氛的驚悚片

fusion.definition: The act of merging separate elements, or the result thereof.
fusion.example: The physicist was aware of the potential danger of nuclear fusion.
fusion.exampleZh: 這位物理學家意識到核融合的潛在危險。

robust.example: A robust wall was put up.
robust.exampleZh: 一堵堅固的牆豎了起來。

robustly.example: France has a robust public transit network.
robustly.exampleZh: 法國擁有強大的公共交通網絡。

crave.definition: To desire strongly, so as to satisfy an appetite; to long or yearn for.

dismally.example: The flyover provides a dismal view of slum dwellings.
dismally.exampleZh: 從天橋上可以看到貧民窟住宅的慘淡景色。

prospective.definition: Likely or expected to happen or become.
prospective.example: Prospective students are those who have already applied to the university, but have yet to be admitted.
prospective.exampleZh: 未來的學生是指已經申請大學但尚未被錄取的學生。

craving.example: The word "craving" is commonly used in formal English.
craving.exampleZh: 「渴望」一詞在正式英語中常用。

remotely.example: I'm not remotely in love with you.

swap.definition: To exchange or give (something) in an exchange (for something else).

splatter.definition: To splash; to scatter; to land or strike in an uneven, distributed mess.
splatter.example: The drink splattered all over me, the table, and the floor when I knocked it over.
splatter.exampleZh: 當我把它打翻時，飲料濺到了我身上、桌子上和地板上。

automatic.example: I never learned to drive a stick. I can only drive an automatic.
automatic.exampleZh: 我從來沒有學過駕駛棍。我只能開自動擋。

bubbly.example: Whip the egg white into a bubbly froth.

violet.exampleZh: 瑪利亞並不是膽怯的人。

apprehensive.definition: Anticipating something with anxiety or fear.

settee.definition: A long seat with a back, made to accommodate several persons at once; a sofa.
settee.example: The pencil has rolled under the settee.
settee.exampleZh: 鉛筆滾到了長沙發底下。

trampoline.definition: A gymnastic and recreational device consisting of a piece of taut, strong fabric stretched over a steel frame using many coiled springs as anchors.

stamina.definition: The energy and strength for continuing to do something over a long period of time; power of sustained exertion, or resistance to hardship, illness etc.
stamina.example: He has a lot of stamina. I suppose that's why he can run for a long time.
stamina.exampleZh: 他有很強的耐力。我想這就是他能跑很久的原因。

ploy.example: The free T-shirt is really a ploy to get you inside to see their sales pitch.
ploy.exampleZh: 免費T卹其實是一種讓你進去看看他們的銷售宣傳的策略。

overalls.definition: A garment worn over other clothing to protect it; a coverall or boiler suit. A garment, for manual labor or for casual wear, often made of a single piece of fabric, with long legs and a bib upper, supported from the shoulders with straps, and having several large pockets and loops for carrying tools.

unison.definition: (acoustics) Identical pitch between two notes or sounds; the simultaneous playing of notes of identical pitch (or separated by one or more octaves).
unison.example: The unison has a pitch ratio of 1:1.

dither.definition: To tremble, shake, or shiver with cold.
dither.example: She tends to dither when faced with choices.
dither.exampleZh: 面對選擇時，她往往會猶豫不決。

snail.definition: Any of very many animals (either hermaphroditic or nonhermaphroditic), of the class Gastropoda, having a coiled shell.
snail.example: The cars were snailing along the motorway during the rush hour.
snail.exampleZh: 尖峰時段，汽車在高速公路上緩慢行駛。

acre.definition: An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day's plowing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840 square yards or 4,046.86 square meters.
acre.example: I like my new house - there’s acres of space!
acre.exampleZh: 我喜歡我的新房子——有幾英畝的空間！

inexhaustible.example: Books are inexhaustible sources of knowledge.
inexhaustible.exampleZh: 書籍是取之不盡、用之不竭的知識來源。

heifer.definition: A young female cow, (particularly) one over one year old but which has not calved.
heifer.example: Once a heifer has a calf, it is no longer a heifer; it is a cow.
heifer.exampleZh: 一旦小母牛生下了小母牛，它就不再是小母牛了；它是一頭牛。

ewe.definition: A female sheep, as opposed to a ram.

adversary.example: He prepared to fight his adversary.
adversary.exampleZh: 他準備與他的對手戰鬥。

profound.definition: Descending far below the surface; opening or reaching to great depth; deep.

victorious.definition: Being the winner in a contest, struggle, war, etc.
victorious.example: The team were met with a victorious cheer.
victorious.exampleZh: 球隊迎來了勝利的歡呼聲。

baldly.example: The word "baldly" is commonly used in formal English.

bald.example: The bald cypress is a tree that loses its leaves in winter.
bald.exampleZh: 禿柏是一種在冬天會落葉的樹。

profoundly.exampleZh: 他的思考和寫作都很深刻。

bearded.definition: To grow hair on the chin and jaw.
bearded.example: Robin Hood is always shown as bearding the Sheriff of Nottingham.
bearded.exampleZh: 羅賓漢總是留著諾丁漢警長的鬍子。

professionalism.definition: The status, methods, character or standards expected of a professional or of a professional organization, such as reliability, discretion, evenhandedness, and fair play.

artistically.example: The word "artistically" is commonly used in formal English.
artistically.exampleZh: 「藝術地」一詞在正式英語中常用。

pendulum.definition: A body suspended from a fixed support so that it swings freely back and forth under the influence of gravity, commonly used to regulate various devices such as clocks.
pendulum.example: Its motion resembles the oscillation of a pendulum.
pendulum.exampleZh: 它的運動類似鐘擺的擺動。

boast.definition: A brag; ostentatious positive appraisal of oneself.
boast.example: It was his regular boast that he could eat two full English breakfasts in one sitting.
boast.exampleZh: 他常吹噓自己可以一口氣吃兩頓全套英式早餐。

predecessor.definition: One who precedes; one who has preceded another in any state, position, office, etc.; one whom another follows or comes after, in any office or position.
predecessor.example: This candidate is significantly better compared to his predecessor.
predecessor.exampleZh: 這位候選人比他的前任好得多。

lapse.example: It was a serious lapse in judgment.
lapse.exampleZh: 這是一個嚴重的判斷失誤。

ceaseless.example: Her ceaseless caterwauling alarmed every dog within a 500-foot radius.
ceaseless.exampleZh: 她不斷的叫聲驚動了方圓 500 英尺內的每隻狗。

ceaselessly.example: He worked ceaselessly to achieve his goals.
ceaselessly.exampleZh: 他為實現自己的目標而不懈努力。

climax.definition: (originally rhetorical) A rhetorical device in which a series is arranged in ascending order.
climax.example: Tom doesn't know the difference between climax and climacteric.
climax.exampleZh: 湯姆不知道高潮和更年期的差別。

climactic.definition: Of, pertaining to, or constituting a climax; reaching a decisive moment or point of greatest tension.
climactic.example: The race ended in a climactic, neck-and-neck scramble for the finish line.
climactic.exampleZh: 比賽在激烈的終點線爭奪中結束。

scramble.definition: A rush or hurry, especially making use of the limbs against a surface.
scramble.example: a last-minute scramble to the finish line

anticlimactic.definition: Lacking climax, disappointing or ironically insignificant following of impressive foreshadowing.
anticlimactic.example: The first half of the movie was captivating, but the ending was anticlimactic.
anticlimactic.exampleZh: 電影的前半段很扣人心弦，但結局卻有些虎頭蛇尾。

twitch.definition: Couch grass (Elymus repens; a species of grass, often considered as a weed)

forfeit.definition: To suffer the loss of something by wrongdoing or non-compliance
forfeit.example: He forfeited his last chance of an early release from jail by repeatedly attacking another inmate.
forfeit.exampleZh: 由於多次襲擊另一名囚犯，他失去了提前出獄的最後機會。

intellect.definition: The faculty of thinking, judging, abstract reasoning, and conceptual understanding; the cognitive faculty (uncountable)
intellect.example: Intellect is one of man's greatest powers.
intellect.exampleZh: 智力是人類最偉大的力量之一。

descent.definition: An instance of descending; act of coming down.
descent.example: We climbed the mountain with difficulty, but the descent was easier.
descent.exampleZh: 我們爬山很困難，但下山就輕鬆多了。

exodus.definition: A sudden departure of a large number of people.
exodus.example: There was an exodus when the show ended.

shed.example: A metal comb shed her golden hair.
shed.exampleZh: 一把金屬梳子梳理著她金色的頭髮。

immortalize.definition: To give unending life to, to make immortal.
immortalize.example: His heroic deeds were immortalised in song and tale.
immortalize.exampleZh: 他的英雄事蹟在歌曲和故事中永垂不朽。

genome.definition: The complete genetic information (either DNA or, in some viruses, RNA) of an organism.
genome.example: The double helix that encodes our genome is an elegant structure.
genome.exampleZh: 編碼我們基因組的雙螺旋是一種優雅的結構。

fraction.definition: A part of a whole, especially a comparatively small part.
fraction.example: Find the reciprocal of this fraction.

faction.definition: A group of people, especially within a political organization, which expresses a shared belief or opinion different from people who are not part of the group.
faction.example: The chief said that this faction cannot be considered dismantled.
faction.exampleZh: 首領說這個派係不能算解散。

utensil.definition: An instrument or device for domestic use, especially in the kitchen.
utensil.example: We have convenient storage for all the kitchen utensils.
utensil.exampleZh: 我們為所有廚房用具提供便利的儲存空間。

physique.definition: The natural constitution, or physical structure, of a person.
physique.example: Yanni is known for his imposing physique.
physique.exampleZh: 雅尼以其雄偉的體格而聞名。

clammy.definition: Cold and damp, usually referring to hands or palms.
clammy.example: His hands were clammy from fright.

otter.definition: An aquatic or marine carnivorous mammal in the subfamily Lutrinae of the family Mustelidae, which also includes weasels, polecats, badgers, and others.
otter.example: The otter features in mythology and folklore across the world.
otter.exampleZh: 水獺出現在世界各地的神話和民間傳說中。

dispense.definition: To issue, distribute, or give out.

absolute.definition: That which is independent of context-dependent interpretation, inviolate, fundamental.

dispensary.definition: A place or room where something is dispensed.
dispensary.example: I thought it was a tea shop, but it was a marijuana dispensary.
dispensary.exampleZh: 我以為這是一家茶館，但它是一家大麻藥局。

diverge.definition: (of lines or paths) To run apart; to separate; to tend into different directions.
diverge.example: Both stories start out the same way, but they diverge halfway through.
diverge.exampleZh: 兩個故事的開頭相同，但中途卻出現了分歧。

indispensable.definition: Not admitting ecclesiastical dispensation; not subject to release or exemption; that cannot be allowed by bending the canonical rules.
indispensable.example: The law was moral and indispensable. -Bp. Burnet
indispensable.exampleZh: 法律是道德的且不可或缺的。 -Bp。伯內特

divergence.definition: The degree to which two or more things diverge.
divergence.example: An angle is made by the divergence of straight lines.
divergence.exampleZh: 角度是由直線的發散形成的。

burst.definition: An act or instance of bursting.
burst.example: The bursts of the bombs could be heard miles away.
burst.exampleZh: 炸彈爆炸的聲音幾英里外都能聽到。

superficial.definition: Of or pertaining to the surface.
superficial.example: He always concentrates on the superficials and fails to see the real issue.
superficial.exampleZh: 他總是只注重表面現象，看不到真正的問題。

superficially.exampleZh: 相似之處純粹是膚淺的。

aesthetic.definition: Concerned with beauty, artistic impact, or appearance.
aesthetic.example: It works well enough, but the shabby exterior offends his aesthetic sensibilities.
aesthetic.exampleZh: 它運作得很好，但破舊的外觀冒犯了他的美學情感。

aesthetically.definition: In an aesthetic manner; with a pleasing sensory effect.
aesthetically.example: These diacritics are unnecessary and aesthetically displeasing.
aesthetically.exampleZh: 這些變音符號是不必要的並且不美觀。

scrutiny.definition: Intense study of someone or something.
scrutiny.example: Corporate political donations are coming under heavy scrutiny.
scrutiny.exampleZh: 企業政治捐款正受到嚴格審查。

quip.definition: A smart, sarcastic turn or jest; a taunt; a severe retort or comeback; a gibe.
quip.example: That quip was just not funny enough.
quip.exampleZh: 那句俏皮話還不夠好笑。

woo.definition: To endeavor to gain someone's support.
woo.example: Algeria needs to woo Western tourists.
woo.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要吸引西方遊客。

criteria.example: Criterion of choice, of decision, of selection

plausible.definition: Seemingly or apparently valid, likely, or acceptable; conceivably true or likely

plausibility.definition: The quality of deserving applause, praiseworthiness; something worthy of praise.
plausibility.example: The word "plausibility" is commonly used in formal English.
plausibility.exampleZh: 「合理性」一詞在正式英語中常用。

plausibly.example: She lied plausibly, but the police suspected her anyway.
plausibly.exampleZh: 她謊話連篇，但警方還是懷疑她。

instinctive.definition: Related to or prompted by instinct.
instinctive.exampleZh: 齊瑞本能地做出了反應。

instinctively.definition: Innately; by instinct; without being taught.
instinctively.example: Fish instinctively know how to swim; most humans don't.
instinctively.exampleZh: 魚本能地知道如何游泳；大多數人沒有。

stylistic.definition: Of or pertaining to style, especially to linguistic or literary style.

stylistically.example: The word "stylistically" is commonly used in formal English.
stylistically.exampleZh: 「風格上」一詞在正式英語中常用。

inheritance.definition: The passing of title to an estate upon death.
inheritance.example: The Indo-European languages share various similarities as a result of their inheritance from a common ancestor.
inheritance.exampleZh: 印歐語係由於繼承自共同的祖先而具有許多相似之處。

inherent.definition: Naturally as part or consequence of something.
inherent.exampleZh: 民族國家本質上是壓迫性的。

tune.definition: To adjust (a musical instrument) so that it produces the correct pitches.
tune.example: to tune a piano or a violin

inherently.definition: In an inherent way; naturally, innately.
inherently.exampleZh: 民族國家本質上是壓迫性的。

spontaneous.definition: Self-generated; happening without any apparent external cause.
spontaneous.example: He made a spontaneous offer of help.
spontaneous.exampleZh: 他自發性地提出提供協助。

spontaneously.definition: In a spontaneous manner; naturally; voluntarily.

honk.example: They stood and observed how long it took for the other cars to honk.
honk.exampleZh: 他們站起來，觀察其他車輛鳴喇叭需要多長時間。

goose.definition: Any of various grazing waterfowl of the family Anatidae, which have feathers and webbed feet and are capable of flying, swimming, and walking on land, and which are bigger than ducks.
goose.example: There is a flock of geese on the pond.

innovate.definition: To alter, to change into something new; to revolutionize.
innovate.example: to innovate a word or an act
innovate.exampleZh: 創新一個字或一個行為

innovative.definition: Characterized by the creation of new ideas or inventions.

innovation.definition: The act of innovating; the introduction of something new, in customs, rites, etc.
innovation.example: The others, whose time had been more actively employed, began to shew symptoms of innovation,—"the good wine did its good office." The frost of etiquette, and pride of birth, began to give way before the genial blessings of this benign constellation, and the formal appellatives with which the three dignitaries had hitherto addressed each other, were now familiarly abbreviated into Tully, Bally, and Killie.
innovation.exampleZh: 其他人的時間得到了更積極的利用，開始表現出創新的跡象——「好酒發揮了它的作用。」在這個仁慈的星座的親切祝福面前，禮儀的嚴酷和出身的驕傲開始消失，三位貴賓迄今為止互相稱呼的正式稱呼現在被人們熟悉地縮寫為塔利、巴利和基利。

abstraction.definition: The act of abstracting, separating, withdrawing, or taking away; withdrawal; the state of being taken away.
abstraction.example: Abstraction is necessary for the classification of things into genera and species.
abstraction.exampleZh: 抽象化對於將事物分類為屬和種是必要的。

connoisseur.definition: A specialist in a given field whose opinion is highly valued, especially in one of the fine arts or in matters of taste
connoisseur.example: Gustavo is a connoisseur of classic movies.
connoisseur.exampleZh: 古斯塔沃是一位經典電影鑑賞家。

tantrum.definition: An often childish display or fit of bad temper.
tantrum.example: Baby Shawn threw a tantrum when he was told the bicycle was not his.
tantrum.exampleZh: 當小肖恩被告知自行車不是他的時，他大發脾氣。

institute.definition: An organization founded to promote a cause
institute.example: I work in a medical research institute.
institute.exampleZh: 我在一家醫學研究所工作。

angle.definition: (often in the passive) To place (something) at an angle.
angle.example: The roof is angled at 15 degrees.

fatality.definition: The state proceeding from destiny; invincible necessity, superior to, and independent of, free and rational control.

stiffen.example: Dad used spray starch to stiffen his dress shirts.
stiffen.exampleZh: 爸爸用噴霧澱粉讓他的正裝襯衫變硬。

flatter.example: The land around here is flat.
flatter.exampleZh: 這裡周圍的土地很平坦。

wooded.example: The word "wooded" is commonly used in formal English.

flattery.definition: Excessive praise or approval, which is often insincere and sometimes contrived to win favour.
flattery.example: She is easily taken in by flattery.
flattery.exampleZh: 她很容易被阿諛奉承所蒙蔽。

aimlessly.definition: Without an aim, purpose or direction; in an aimless manner.
aimlessly.example: He wandered aimlessly through the crowd.
aimlessly.exampleZh: 他漫無目的地在人群中徘徊。

meaningless.example: The word "gugugu" is meaningless in English.
meaningless.exampleZh: 「gugugu」這個字在英文中是沒有意義的。

candid.example: His portraits looked stiff and formal but his candids showed life being lived.
candid.exampleZh: 他的肖像畫看起來僵硬而正式，但他的坦率表現了真實的生活。

provoke.definition: To cause someone to become annoyed or angry.
provoke.example: Don't provoke the dog; it may try to bite you.
provoke.exampleZh: 不要激怒狗；它可能會試圖咬你。

vitality.definition: The capacity to live and develop.

provocative.definition: Serving or tending to elicit a strong, often negative sentiment in another person; exasperating.
provocative.example: Provocative clothing is banned at school.
provocative.exampleZh: 學校禁止穿著挑釁性服裝。

provocatively.example: Provocative clothing is banned at school.
provocatively.exampleZh: 學校禁止穿著挑釁性服裝。

clench.example: He clenched his fist in anger.

haggle.definition: To argue for a better deal, especially over prices with a seller.
haggle.example: I haggled for a better price because the original price was too high.
haggle.exampleZh: 我討價還價以獲得更好的價格，因為原價太高了。

scornful.exampleZh: 湯姆做出一臉輕蔑的表情。

obscure.definition: To render obscure; to darken; to make dim; to keep in the dark; to hide; to make less visible, intelligible, legible, glorious, beautiful, or illustrious.
obscure.example: an obscure passage or inscription;    The speaker made obscure references to little-known literary works.
obscure.exampleZh: 晦澀的段落或銘文；    演講者含糊地提到了鮮為人知的文學作品。

elevation.definition: The act of raising from a lower place, condition, or quality to a higher; said of material things, persons, the mind, the voice, etc.
elevation.example: the elevation of grain; elevation to a throne; elevation to sainthood; elevation of mind, thoughts, or character
elevation.exampleZh: 穀物的海拔；登上王位；晉升為聖人；心靈、思想或品格的提升

elevate.definition: To raise (something) to a higher position.
elevate.example: The doctor told me elevating my legs would help reduce the swelling.
elevate.exampleZh: 醫生告訴我抬高雙腿可以幫助減輕腫脹。

pose.exampleZh: 我可以再問一個問題嗎？

succession.definition: An act of following in sequence.
succession.exampleZh: 財務上的成功隨之而來。

jog.definition: To push slightly; to move or shake with a push or jerk, as to gain the attention of; to jolt.

pioneer.definition: To be the first to do or achieve (something), preparing the way for others to follow.
pioneer.example: The young doctor pioneered a new life-saving surgical technique.
pioneer.exampleZh: 這位年輕的醫生開創了一種新的救生手術技術。

mountaineer.definition: A person who climbs mountains for sport or pleasure.

quest.definition: A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.
quest.example: to rove in quest of game, of a lost child, of property, etc.
quest.exampleZh: 四處遊蕩尋找遊戲、迷失的孩子、財產等。

transatlantic.definition: On, spanning or crossing, or from the other side of the Atlantic Ocean.
transatlantic.example: Lindbergh's solo nonstop transatlantic flight was a remarkable accomplishment.
transatlantic.exampleZh: 林德伯格的單人不間斷跨大西洋飛行是一項了不起的成就。

rudiments.definition: (often in the plural) A fundamental principle or skill, especially in a field of learning.
rudiments.example: We'll be learning the rudiments of thermodynamics next week.
rudiments.exampleZh: 下週我們將學習熱力學的基礎知識。

rudimentary.definition: Of or relating to one or more rudiments.
rudimentary.example: I have only a rudimentary grasp of chemistry.
rudimentary.exampleZh: 我對化學只有初步的了解。

paddle.definition: To propel something through water with a paddle, oar, hands, etc.
paddle.example: We had a nice paddle this morning.
paddle.exampleZh: 今天早上我們劃了一個很好的槳。

immensely.definition: Greatly; hugely; extremely; vastly; to a great extent.

advisory.example: The advisory committee could only offer advice, but since that was almost always accepted they had real power.
advisory.exampleZh: 諮詢委員會只能提供建議，但由於建議幾乎總是被接受，因此他們擁有實權。

trying.example: I tried to rollerblade, but I couldn’t.
trying.exampleZh: 我嘗試滑旱冰，但我做不到。

adrenaline.exampleZh: 我的腎上腺素真的在飆升。

tinker.definition: To fiddle with something in an attempt to fix, mend or improve it, especially in an experimental or unskilled manner.
tinker.example: Tom likes to tinker with things because he believes there's always a better way.
tinker.exampleZh: 湯姆喜歡修補東西，因為他相信總有更好的方法。

lavatory.definition: A vessel or fixture for washing, particularly:
lavatory.example: Tom got caught smoking in the airplane lavatory.
lavatory.exampleZh: 湯姆在飛機廁所吸煙被抓。

insecure.example: He's a nice guy and all, but seems to be rather insecure around other people.
insecure.exampleZh: 他是個好人，但在其他人面前似乎相當缺乏安全感。

pinnacle.example: Is man really the pinnacle of Creation, or just a freak of nature?
pinnacle.exampleZh: 人類真的是受造物的巔峰，還是只是大自然的怪物？

reassure.definition: To assure anew; to restore confidence to; to free from fear or self-doubt.
reassure.example: The boy's mother reassured him that there was no monster hiding under the bed.
reassure.exampleZh: 男孩的母親向他保證床底下沒有藏著怪物。

reassurance.definition: The feeling of being reassured, of having confidence restored, of having apprehensions dispelled.
reassurance.example: The word "reassurance" is commonly used in formal English.
reassurance.exampleZh: 「放心」一詞在正式英語中常用。

drawback.definition: A disadvantage; something that detracts or takes away.
drawback.example: Poor fuel economy is a common drawback among larger vehicles.
drawback.exampleZh: 燃油經濟性差是大型車輛的常見缺點。

profess.definition: To administer the vows of a religious order to (someone); to admit to a religious order. (Chiefly in passive.)

oversee.definition: To survey, look at something in a wide angle.
oversee.example: It is congress's duty to oversee the spending of federal funds.
oversee.exampleZh: 監督聯邦資金的支出是國會的職責。

resort.definition: To have recourse (to), now especially from necessity or frustration.

overseer.example: She usually wears oversized clothes in shades of gray.
overseer.exampleZh: 她通常穿著大號的灰色衣服。

estimation.definition: The process of making an estimate.

liaison.definition: Communication between two parties or groups.
liaison.example: Administrators need to establish a close liaison with employees.
liaison.exampleZh: 管理者需要與員工建立密切的聯繫。

notwithstanding.example: He, notwithstanding, persisted in his inquiries.
notwithstanding.exampleZh: 儘管如此，他還是堅持詢問。

loom.definition: A utensil; tool; a weapon; (usually in compound) an article in general.
loom.example: The Kabyle loom is very old.

structurally.example: After the hurricane, the building was structurally sound but aesthetically displeasing.
structurally.exampleZh: 颶風過後，這棟建築結構完好，但美觀程度令人不快。

structural.definition: Of, relating to, or having structure.
structural.exampleZh: 阿爾及利亞需要結構性改革。

rapport.definition: A relationship of mutual trust and respect. A close and harmonious relationship in which the people or groups concerned understand each other's feelings or ideas and communicate well.
rapport.example: He always tried to maintain a rapport with his customers.
rapport.exampleZh: 他總是盡力與顧客保持融洽的關係。

sedentary.definition: Not moving; relatively still; staying in the vicinity.
sedentary.example: The oyster is a sedentary mollusk; the barnacles are sedentary crustaceans.
sedentary.exampleZh: 牡蠣是一種久坐的軟體動物。藤壺是定居的甲殼類動物。

consultation.example: The word "consultation" is commonly used in formal English.
consultation.exampleZh: 「諮詢」一詞在正式英語中常用。

landscape.definition: To create or maintain a landscape.
landscape.example: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years
landscape.exampleZh: 軟體專利格局在過去幾年中發生了很大變化

bloom.definition: A blossom; the flower of a plant; an expanded bud.
bloom.example: The cherry trees are in bloom.

consultancy.example: For a PR campaign like never before, one needs a consultancy of unprecedented scope and power.
consultancy.exampleZh: 對於一場前所未有的公關活動，需要一家規模和實力前所未見的顧問公司。

engineer.definition: To design, construct or manage something as an engineer.

quarry.definition: A site for mining stone, limestone, or slate.
quarry.example: Michelangelo personally quarried marble from the world-famous quarry at Carrara.
quarry.exampleZh: 米開朗基羅親自從世界著名的卡拉拉採石場開採大理石。

retention.definition: The act of retaining or something retained
retention.example: Language learning stimulates brain activity and improves memory retention.
retention.exampleZh: 語言學習可以刺激大腦活動並提高記憶力。

specification.definition: An explicit set of requirements to be satisfied by a material, product, or service.
specification.example: The word "specification" is commonly used in formal English.
specification.exampleZh: 「規範」一詞在正式英語中常用。

query.example: The teacher answered the student’s query concerning biosynthesis.
query.exampleZh: 老師解答了學生關於生物合成的疑問。

contractor.definition: A person or company that builds or improves buildings.
contractor.example: Tom hired a local contractor to remodel his kitchen.
contractor.exampleZh: 湯姆聘請了當地的承包商來改造他的廚房。

chill.definition: To lower the temperature of something; to cool

possessor.definition: Agent noun of possess; one who possesses
possessor.example: Knowledge is a treasure that goes everywhere with its possessor.
possessor.exampleZh: 知識是一種寶藏，擁有者就可以隨身攜帶。

chilly.example: I’m getting rather chilly over here – could you shut the window please?
chilly.exampleZh: 我這裡有點冷——你能把窗戶關上嗎？

frost.example: I think the boss's decision frosted him a bit.
frost.exampleZh: 我認為老闆的決定讓他有點受挫。

man.example: The show is especially popular with middle-aged men.
man.exampleZh: 該節目尤其受到中年男性的歡迎。

unmanned.definition: Not operated by a person or a crew
unmanned.example: an automated e-mail message sent from an unmanned mailbox
unmanned.exampleZh: 從無人值守的郵箱發送的自動電子郵件

impersonal.definition: Not personal; not representing a person; not having personality.
impersonal.example: She sounded impersonal as she gave her report of the Nazi death camps.
impersonal.exampleZh: 當她講述納粹死亡集中營的情況時，她的語氣聽起來毫無人情味。

impersonate.definition: To pretend to be (a different person); to assume the identity of.
impersonate.example: The conman managed to impersonate several executives.
impersonate.exampleZh: 該騙子設法冒充幾名高階主管。

impersonator.definition: One who fraudulently impersonates another person.

buzzer.definition: One who, or that which, buzzes; an insect that buzzes.
buzzer.example: If you think you know the answer to the question, hit the buzzer as fast as you can.
buzzer.exampleZh: 如果您認為您知道問題的答案，請盡快按下蜂鳴器。

impersonation.example: Tom is a master of impersonation.

desert.definition: (usually in the plural) That which is deserved or merited; a just punishment or reward

stuffy.example: I can’t smell very well today – I have a stuffy nose.
stuffy.exampleZh: 我今天聞起來不太好——我鼻子不通氣。

unoccupied.definition: (of a house etc) Not inhabited, especially by a tenant
unoccupied.example: Paul checked the stall to see that it was unoccupied.
unoccupied.exampleZh: 保羅檢查了攤位，發現沒有人。

gape.definition: To open the mouth wide, especially involuntarily, as in a yawn, anger, or surprise.
gape.example: I can only gape at such perfection.
gape.exampleZh: 我只能目瞪口呆地看著這樣的完美。

repute.definition: To attribute or credit something to something; to impute.
repute.exampleZh: 卡拜爾編織，馬爾布特享有盛名。

recall.definition: The action or fact of calling someone or something back.
recall.example: He was recalled to service after his retirement.

toughness.example: The word "toughness" is commonly used in formal English.

recollection.definition: The act of recollecting, or recalling to the memory; the act of recalling to memory
recollection.example: Alas that distant event isn't within my recollection anymore.
recollection.exampleZh: 唉，那遙遠的事已經不在我的記憶中了。

plumbing.definition: The pipes, together with the joints, tanks, stopcocks, taps and other fixtures of a water, gas or sewage system in a house or other building.
plumbing.example: My plumbing was playing up, so I had to see the doctor.
plumbing.exampleZh: 我的水管出了問題，所以我必須去看醫生。

usher.definition: A person, in a church, cinema etc., who escorts people to their seats.
usher.example: The usher showed us to our seats.
usher.exampleZh: 引座員帶我們到座位上。

glum.example: Robert looked glum for a long time.
glum.exampleZh: 羅伯特鬱悶了很長一段時間。

gamely.definition: In a game or plucky manner; in a willing and spirited fashion.
gamely.example: The word "gamely" is commonly used in formal English.

dissect.definition: To study an animal's anatomy by cutting it apart; to perform a necropsy or an autopsy.

commend.example: The schoolboy was commended for raising the alarm about the burning building.
commend.exampleZh: 這名男學生因就著火的建築物發出警報而受到讚揚。

dissection.definition: The act of dissecting, or something dissected
dissection.exampleZh: 裡瑪和斯庫拉解剖青蛙。

commendation.definition: The act of commending; praise; favorable representation in words; recommendation.
commendation.example: The word "commendation" is commonly used in formal English.
commendation.exampleZh: 「讚揚」一詞在正式英語中常用。

flick.definition: To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.

flicker.example: the flicker of a poorly tuned television set
flicker.exampleZh: 調諧不良的電視機的閃爍

thereby.definition: By it; by that; by that means, or as a consequence of that.
thereby.example: The failure was detected in time, thereby preventing a disaster.
thereby.exampleZh: 及時發現故障，避免了災難的發生。

voice.definition: Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character
voice.example: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.
voice.exampleZh: 他低沉的聲音使他成為合唱團的貝斯手。

tread.definition: To step or walk (on or over something); to trample.

unenviable.definition: Difficult, undesirable, or unpleasant; not to be envied.
unenviable.example: Valjean was in the unenviable position of either living with the knowledge an innocent would suffer for his crime or revealing his true identity and going back to prison.
unenviable.exampleZh: 冉阿讓處於令人羨慕的境地，要麼生活在無辜者因犯罪而遭受痛苦的知識中，要麼揭露自己的真實身份並回到監獄。

allege.definition: To state under oath, to plead.
allege.example: The agency alleged my credit history had problems.
allege.exampleZh: 該機構聲稱我的信用記錄有問題。

bribe.definition: To give a bribe to; specifically, to ask a person to do something, usually against his/her will, in exchange for some type of reward or relief from potential trouble.
bribe.example: She was accused of trying to bribe the jury into making false statements.
bribe.exampleZh: 她被指控試圖賄賂陪審團做出虛假陳述。

inclusion.definition: An addition or annex to a group, set, or total.
inclusion.example: The poem was a new inclusion in the textbook.
inclusion.exampleZh: 這首詩被新列入教科書。

bribery.definition: The making of illegal payment, or bribes, to persons in official positions as a means of influencing their decisions
bribery.example: This is what is called a 'present' in some countries and 'bribery' in others.
bribery.exampleZh: 這在一些國家被稱為“禮物”，在其他國家被稱為“賄賂”。

inclusive.example: an inclusive list of data formats

unconvincing.example: He gave me an unconvincing explanation of the fault, but I have my doubts.
unconvincing.exampleZh: 他給了我一個令人信服的錯誤解釋，但我有疑問。

recharge.definition: To charge an electric battery after its power has been consumed.
recharge.example: My phone has needed five recharges in the last month.
recharge.exampleZh: 我的手機上個月需要充電五次。

facilitation.definition: The act of facilitating or making easy.
facilitation.example: Does networked administration facilitate interministerial coordination?
facilitation.exampleZh: 網路化管理是否有利於部際協調？

condition.definition: To subject to the process of acclimation.
condition.example: I became conditioned to the absence of seasons in San Diego.
condition.exampleZh: 我已經習慣了聖地牙哥沒有季節的情況。

conditional.example: I made my son a conditional promise: I would buy him a bike if he kept his room tidy.
conditional.exampleZh: 我向兒子提出了一個有條件的承諾：如果他保持房間整潔，我就買一輛腳踏車給他。

tone.definition: To give a particular tone to
tone.example: Her rousing speech gave an upbeat tone to the rest of the evening.
tone.exampleZh: 她激動人心的演講為當晚剩下的時間帶來了樂觀的基調。

conditionally.example: The word "conditionally" is commonly used in formal English.
conditionally.exampleZh: 「有條件地」一詞在正式英語中常用。

manicure.definition: A cosmetic treatment for the fingernails.
manicure.example: Sami liked to manicure his lawn.
manicure.exampleZh: 薩米喜歡修剪他的草坪。

facial.example: The facial constitutionality of the law is in question.
facial.exampleZh: 該法表面上的合憲性受到質疑。

pedicure.definition: Superficial cosmetic treatment of the feet and toenails.
pedicure.example: When was the last time you got a pedicure?
pedicure.exampleZh: 您最後一次修腳是什麼時候？

bouquet.example: For my birthday I received two bouquets.
bouquet.exampleZh: 在我的生日那天，我收到了兩束花。

refurbish.definition: To rebuild or replenish with all new material; to restore to original (or better) working order and appearance.
refurbish.example: David took a loan to refurbish his home.
refurbish.exampleZh: 大衛借了一筆貸款來翻新他的房子。

grounds.definition: The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.
grounds.example: Look, I found a ten dollar bill on the ground!
grounds.exampleZh: 看，我在地上發現了一張十美元的鈔票！

refurbishment.example: That ship was towed into the shipyard for refurbishment.
refurbishment.exampleZh: 那艘船被拖到造船廠進行整修。

lethargy.definition: A condition characterized by extreme fatigue or drowsiness, or prolonged sleep patterns.
lethargy.example: Lethargy is a common symptom of that disease.
lethargy.exampleZh: 嗜睡是該疾病的常見症狀。

ground.definition: To connect (an electrical conductor or device) to a ground.
ground.example: Eric, you are grounded until further notice for lying to us about where you were last night!
ground.exampleZh: 艾瑞克，你因向我們謊報你昨晚在哪裡而被禁足，直至另行通知！

lethargic.example: The forum looks lethargic; maybe it's your diet.
lethargic.exampleZh: 論壇看起來毫無生氣；也許是你的飲食習慣。

stately.definition: Of people: worthy of respect; dignified, regal.

conserve.definition: To save for later use, sometimes by the use of a preservative.

disquieting.definition: Causing mental trouble or anguish; upsetting; making uneasy.
disquieting.example: He felt disquieted by the lack of interest the child had shown.
disquieting.exampleZh: 他對孩子表現出的缺乏興趣感到不安。

conservatory.example: Tchaikovsky enrolled at the Saint Petersburg Conservatory.
conservatory.exampleZh: 柴可夫斯基就讀於聖彼得堡音樂學院。

fiercely.example: The wind blew fiercely and the rain fell heavily.
fiercely.exampleZh: 風吹得很猛，雨下得很大。

grapefruit.definition: The tree of the species Citrus paradisi, a hybrid of pomelo (Citrus maxima) and sweet orange.
grapefruit.exampleZh: 葡萄柚會影響我的藥物嗎？

spacious.example: The apartment has a spacious bedroom.

flask.definition: A narrow-necked vessel of metal or glass, used for various purposes; as of sheet metal, to carry gunpowder in; or of wrought iron, to contain quicksilver; or of glass, to heat water in, etc.

gourmet.definition: (of food and drink) Fine; of superior quality.
gourmet.example: The restaurant offered gourmet coffee and cigars after the meal.
gourmet.exampleZh: 餐後餐廳提供美味的咖啡和雪茄。

rich.definition: Wealthy: having a lot of money and possessions.
rich.example: a rich dish; rich cream or soup; rich pastry
rich.exampleZh: 豐盛的菜餚；濃鬱的奶油或湯；豐富的糕點

conjure.example: He started conjuring at the age of 15, and is now a famous stage magician.
conjure.exampleZh: 他15歲開始魔術，現在是一位著名的舞台魔術師。

sauna.definition: A room or a house designed for heat sessions.
sauna.example: The hotel has a sauna in the basement.
sauna.exampleZh: 飯店的地下室設有桑拿浴室。

posture.definition: The way a person holds and positions their body.
posture.example: If you're finished posturing in front of the mirror, can I use the bathroom now?
posture.exampleZh: 如果你在鏡子前擺好了姿勢，現在可以去趟浴室嗎？

clutch.definition: To seize, as though with claws.

sedate.definition: To calm or put (a person) to sleep using a sedative drug.
sedate.exampleZh: 米羅斯拉夫被開了一些鎮靜劑。

stagger.example: the stagger of a drunken man

staggering.example: The army suffered a staggering defeat.

dutiful.definition: Accepting of one's legal or moral obligations and willing to do them well, and without complaint.
dutiful.example: Ralph was a dutiful child, and took the trash out without being told.
dutiful.exampleZh: 拉爾夫是個孝順的孩子，他不經吩咐就把垃圾倒了出去。

dutifully.definition: With a regard to duty; in a dutiful manner.

calorie.definition: (nutrition) Kilogram calorie or large calorie. A unit of energy 1000 times larger than the gram calorie. It is equivalent to the gram kilocalorie, approximately 4.2 kilojoules.
calorie.example: You should check your calorie intake.
calorie.exampleZh: 您應該檢查您的卡路里攝取量。

firm.definition: A business partnership; the name under which it trades.

eclectic.definition: Selecting a mixture of what appears to be best of various doctrines, methods or styles.
eclectic.example: Her eclectic taste was evident from the unusual decor of the room.
eclectic.exampleZh: 從房間不尋常的裝飾中可以看出她不拘一格的品味。

premises.definition: Land, and all the built structures on it, especially when considered as a single place.

batter.definition: To hit or strike violently and repeatedly.
batter.example: The firemen battered down the door.

revere.definition: To regard someone or something with great awe or devotion.

battery.definition: A device used to power electric devices, consisting of a set of electrically connected electrochemical or, archaically, electrostatic cells. A single such cell when used by itself.
battery.example: Schoolchildren take a battery of standard tests to measure their progress.
battery.exampleZh: 小學生要參加一系列標準測驗來衡量他們的進步。

waterfront.definition: The land alongside a body of water.
waterfront.example: We enjoy a jaunt to the wonderful, waterfront city of Portsmouth.
waterfront.exampleZh: 我們享受前往美麗的海濱城市朴茨茅斯的短途旅行。

amplification.definition: The act, or result of amplifying, enlarging, extending or adding.
amplification.example: The amplification of DNA via PCR is a necessary step for many kinds of tests.
amplification.exampleZh: 以 PCR 擴增 DNA 是多種測試的必要步驟。

battered.definition: Beaten up through a lot of use; in rough condition; weathered.
battered.example: The firemen battered down the door.

amply.example: As George Bush has amply demonstrated, being president of the U.S. requires only a modicum of intelligence.
amply.exampleZh: 正如喬治布希充分證明的那樣，成為美國總統只需要一點智力。

comply.definition: To yield assent; to accord; to acquiesce, agree, consent; to adapt oneself, to conform.

compliant.definition: Willing to comply; submissive; willing to do what someone wants.
compliant.example: Tendon is compliant compared to bone.
compliant.exampleZh: 與骨頭相比，肌腱具有順應性。

compliance.example: Compliance with a robber's demands does not guarantee your safety.
compliance.exampleZh: 遵守強盜的要求並不能保證您的安全。

exuberant.definition: (of people) Very high-spirited; extremely energetic and enthusiastic.

exuberance.definition: The quality of being exuberant; cheerful or vigorous enthusiasm; liveliness.
exuberance.example: What exuberance in gesture and tone!
exuberance.exampleZh: 手勢和語氣多麼豐富啊！

linear.definition: Having the form of a line; straight or roughly straight; following a direct course.

flourish.definition: A dramatic gesture such as the waving of a flag.
flourish.example: With many flourishes of the captured banner, they marched down the avenue.
flourish.exampleZh: 他們高舉奪取的旗幟，沿著大道行進。

linearly.example: The effect related linearly to the amount of causative agent added.
linearly.exampleZh: 此效應與添加的致病劑的量呈線性相關。

anchored.definition: Having the extremities turned back, like the flukes of an anchor.

dominant.example: The dominant party controlled the government.

domination.example: The soldiers took cover behind a ruined building.
domination.exampleZh: 士兵們躲在一棟廢墟後面。

interjection.definition: (grammar) An exclamation or filled pause; a word or phrase with no particular grammatical relation to a sentence, often an expression of emotion.

interject.definition: To insert something between other things.

gush.definition: To flow forth suddenly, in great volume.
gush.example: The word "gush" is commonly used in formal English.

voracious.definition: Wanting or devouring great quantities of food.
voracious.example: I know that many Japanese are voracious readers.
voracious.exampleZh: 我知道許多日本人都是如飢似渴的讀者。

sketch.definition: A rapidly executed freehand drawing that is not intended as a finished work, often consisting of a multitude of overlapping lines.

prop.definition: (sometimes figurative) To support or shore up something.
prop.example: Try using a phone book to prop up the table where the foot is missing.
prop.exampleZh: 試著用電話簿支撐桌子缺腳的地方。

sketchbook.definition: A book or pad with blank pages for sketching; a sketch pad.
sketchbook.example: This sketchbook is not good for alcohol markers.
sketchbook.exampleZh: 這本素描本不適合酒精標記。

imp.definition: A small, mischievous sprite, or a malevolent supernatural creature, somewhat comparable to a demon but smaller and less powerful.
imp.example: That pesky imp must be punished.
imp.exampleZh: 那個討厭的小鬼必須受到懲罰。

impish.definition: Mischievous; of or befitting an imp.
impish.example: That impish man over there is my ex-husband.
impish.exampleZh: 那邊那個頑皮的男人是我的前夫。

wicked.example: That was a wicked guitar solo, bro!
wicked.exampleZh: 那是一首邪惡的吉他獨奏，兄弟！

gusto.example: He sang with more gusto than talent.
gusto.exampleZh: 他唱歌的熱情多於才華。

tantamount.definition: Equivalent in meaning or effect; amounting to the same thing in practical terms, even if being technically distinct.
tantamount.example: In this view, disagreement and treason are tantamount.
tantamount.exampleZh: 在這個觀點中，分歧和叛國就等於叛國。

macabre.example: How can you watch such macabre movies without having nightmares?
macabre.exampleZh: 看這麼恐怖的電影怎麼能不做惡夢呢？

relapse.definition: To fall back again; to slide or turn back into a former state or practice.
relapse.example: He has improved recently but keeps relapsing into states of utter confusion.
relapse.exampleZh: 他最近有所好轉，但仍陷入完全混亂的狀態。

antics.definition: A grotesque representation of a figure; a gargoyle.
